findings.claim: Two of the three exported L1 entry points (ptr_app, ptr_app_grid) are thin wrappers that delegate to an unexported *_components builder returning list(ui=, server=) then wrap it in shiny::shinyApp(); ptr_app_bslib has NO components builder and inlines its own bslib ui/server.
findings.detail: ptr_app (R/paintr-app.R:134-152) calls ptr_app_components (154-181) returning list(ui,server) (180), wrapped via shiny::shinyApp(parts$ui,parts$server) (151). ptr_app_grid (1284-1305) calls ptr_app_grid_components (1307-1502) returning list(ui,server) (1494-1501). The components builders are the reusable seam: ptr_app_components takes an extra ns= (default shiny::NS(NULL), 159) for namespaced embedding. ptr_app_bslib (R/paintr-app-bslib.R:56-122) composes the public bare pieces (ptr_ui_controls/ptr_ui_plot/ptr_ui_toggle_code/ptr_ui_inline_error/ptr_ui_error/ptr_ui_code/ptr_server) directly into a bslib::page_sidebar ui + inline server closure and calls shinyApp itself.
findings.verification: R/paintr-app.R: ptr_app (now ~134-152) calls ptr_app_components which returns list(ui=, server=) and wraps via shiny::shinyApp(parts$ui, parts$server). ptr_app_grid (~1256-1275) calls ptr_app_grid_components which builds list(ui,server). ptr_app_components carries ns = shiny::NS(NULL) (line 159). ptr_app_bslib (R/paintr-app-bslib.R, 55-121) has NO components builder: it inlines bslib::page_sidebar UI composing ptr_ui_assets/ptr_ui_controls/ptr_ui_toggle_code/ptr_ui_inline_error/ptr_ui_plot/ptr_ui_error/ptr_ui_code and a plain server closure calling ptr_server, then shinyApp itself. Claim holds; line numbers drifted (now ~134-181, ~1256-1275, ~1277+; bslib 55-121).

findings.claim: ptr_app's only required argument is `formula`; everything else (envir, ui_text, expr_check, safe_to_remove, css, spec) is optional with defaults, and data is supplied implicitly by resolving symbols in `envir` (default parent.frame()) or via a ppUpload placeholder — there is no explicit `data=` parameter.
findings.detail: Signature R/paintr-app.R:134-140: formula has no default; envir=parent.frame(), ui_text=NULL, expr_check=TRUE, safe_to_remove=character(), css=NULL, spec=NULL. Data flows two ways: a dataset named in the formula resolved from envir at render time, or a ppUpload placeholder via file upload. expr_check=TRUE runs the denylist+AST walker on ppExpr; FALSE disables, or a list with deny_list/allow_list. `spec` is a named list of fully-qualified Shiny input id->value to override widget defaults at boot (ADR 0012). Same optional-arg shape repeats across ptr_app_bslib (app-bslib.R:56-62) and ptr_server (app.R:1092-1093).
findings.verification: R/paintr-app.R ptr_app signature: formula (no default), envir=parent.frame(), ui_text=NULL, expr_check=TRUE, safe_to_remove=character(), css=NULL, spec=NULL. No data= parameter; data flows from envir symbol resolution or a ppUpload placeholder (confirmed by roxygen Formula-grammar section and ppUpload source-role wiring). expr_check roxygen documents TRUE/FALSE/list(deny_list,allow_list). Same optional shape on ptr_app_bslib (app-bslib.R 55-61) and ptr_server (app.R 822-829). Holds.

findings.claim: The L1/L2 entry points accept `formula` as EITHER a single string OR an unquoted ggplot expression: ptr_capture_formula() captures via rlang::enexpr(), unwraps a top-level {} block, resolves a bare symbol bound to a string, force-evaluates string-builder/subscript calls (paste/paste0/sprintf/gettextf/format/formatC/c/vector/[/[[/$), unwraps expr/quo/quote/bquote one layer, then deparses any remaining language object via rlang::expr_text(width=500).
findings.detail: ptr_capture_formula (R/paintr-app.R:196-296) is the shared public-boundary dispatch called by ptr_app (141), ptr_app_components (162), ptr_server (1094). Order: unwrap top-level {} (202-206); string passthrough (207-209); bare symbol resolved via eval_bare + rev(sys.frames()) walk accepting string/call/symbol (210-250); expr/quo/quote/bquote unwrapped one layer (261-263); string-builder head (paste/paste0/sprintf/gettextf/format/formatC/c/vector/[/[[/$) force-evaluated, single string else abort (264-287); anything else still a call/symbol deparsed via rlang::expr_text(width=500L) (289-291). GOTCHA (roxygen 116-119): in expression mode R desugars |> before capture, so rendered code shows nested-call form — stay in string mode or use %>% to preserve |>.
findings.verification: Re-read ptr_capture_formula (R/paintr-app.R, def at line 196). Behavior unchanged: top-level {} unwrap (keeps last sub-expr); string passthrough; bare symbol resolved via sys.frames walk accepting string/call/symbol; expr/quo/quote/bquote unwrapped one layer (head_name %in% c("expr","quo","quote","bquote")); string-builder/subscript heads c("paste","paste0","sprintf","gettextf","format","formatC","c","vector","[","[[","$") force-evaluated (single string else abort); remaining call/symbol deparsed via rlang::expr_text(width=500L). Dispatch via rlang::enexpr(formula) at ptr_app (141) and ptr_app_components (162); also ptr_server. Roxygen native-pipe |> desugar caveat present (R/paintr-app.R:11-14). No ADR-0027 arg-rename or fix-expr change touched this function's contract.

findings.claim: ptr_app_grid takes `plots` (a list of formula strings) laid out in an ncol×nrow Bootstrap grid where each tile is a namespaced ptr_ui() module (id 'plot_<i>'); if ANY formula declares a shared value OR shared-consumer placeholder it builds ONE ptr_shared() coordinator as the single source of truth for the cross-formula partition, driving a top-level ptr_shared_panel() + a single Draw-all button across all plots.
findings.detail: ptr_app_grid_components (R/paintr-app.R:1307-1502): coerce+assert plots are strings (1318-1323); any_shared = any tree with collect_shared_placeholders OR collect_shared_consumer_occurrences (1333-1336). If any_shared, obj<-ptr_shared(formulas=plots,...) (1350-1357) is the SINGLE source of truth; ptr_shared_panel(obj) renders the top panel; ptr_shared_server(obj) the reactive bundle. plot_module_ids=paste0('plot_',seq_along(plots)); each tile is ptr_ui(plots[[i]], id, shared=obj). Layout: ncol/nrow facet_wrap-style; col_width=max(1L,12L %/% ncol). Server loops ptr_server per plot forwarding shared_state=state; combined spec via ptr_spec_combine. Shared widget id is canonical_shared_id(key)='shared_<key>'.
findings.verification: Re-read ptr_app_grid_components (R/paintr-app.R, def now at line 1347) and ptr_app_grid (1324). Behavior unchanged: coerce plots to list + assert character (is.character->as.list, assertthat::assert_that); any_shared = any(collect_shared_placeholders(tr)>0 || collect_shared_consumer_occurrences(tr)>0); if any_shared, obj<-ptr_shared(...) single source of truth, ptr_shared_panel(obj) top panel; plot_module_ids<-paste0("plot_",seq_along(plots)); ncol/nrow facet_wrap-style auto-resolve. The claim itself does not assert public/exported status (ptr_app_grid is now @keywords internal at line 1323, but the claim only describes its mechanics), so it still holds as written.

findings.claim: ptr_app_bslib is an UNEXPORTED (@keywords internal as of v0.11.0/ADR-0027), demonstration-only wrapper: it requireNamespace-gates bslib, defaults theme to bslib::bs_theme(version=5, bootswatch='flatly'), hardcodes id='ptr', and CANNOT do single-formula ppVar(shared='...') coordination (its ptr_ui_controls passes shared=NULL) — for that it directs users to ptr_app() or ptr_app_grid().
findings.detail: R/paintr-app-bslib.R:56-122: aborts if bslib missing (63-67); theme default flatly BS5 (68-70); id<-'ptr' (71). Roxygen (1-21) frames it as the copy-and-adapt wrapper pattern, names ptr_app/ptr_app_grid as primary, states single-formula shared unsupported here. theme= is a downstream-library passthrough (page_sidebar 77), not ggpaintr public surface. Wraps both sidebar controls and card body in div.ptr-app + ptr_ui_assets() (deduped page-wide). Server is a plain closure ptr_server(formula,id='ptr',...). shared=NULL on ptr_ui_controls (92) is why single-formula shared is unsupported.
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-app-bslib.R (ptr_app_bslib, def at line 55) and NAMESPACE. Mechanics still hold: requireNamespace('bslib') gate (62-66), default theme bslib::bs_theme(version=5, bootswatch='flatly') (67-69), id<-'ptr' (70), ptr_ui_controls(..., shared=NULL) (91) so single-formula shared unsupported, directs to ptr_app()/ptr_app_grid(). BUT the claim's lead word 'exported' has DRIFTED: per ADR-0027/0.11.0 the function now carries @keywords internal (R/paintr-app-bslib.R:54) and is NOT in NAMESPACE (grep export(ptr_app_bslib) -> absent). Rewriting to drop the false export claim.

findings.claim: ptr_app's UI (ptr_build_app_ui) is a fluidPage with a `.ptr-app.ptr-app--page` div, a branded ptr_ui_header, and a sidebarLayout of ptr_controls_panel beside ptr_outputs_panel; its server (ptr_make_app_server) wires ptr_server_internal (auto_bind_shared=TRUE) plus host-side shared-consumer binding via ptr_bind_local_shared_consumers with host_owned_keys=character(0) (single-instance owns every shared key, no coordinator).
findings.detail: ptr_build_app_ui (758-792): fluidPage(ptr_assets(css), div.ptr-app.ptr-app--page > [header, sidebarLayout(sidebarPanel(ptr_controls_panel), mainPanel(ptr_outputs_panel))]). The --page modifier marks ptr_app as a full-viewport entrypoint; embeddable ptr_ui omits it. ptr_controls_panel (401-438): shared section (gated), pickerInput ptr_layer_select, hidden tabsetPanel ptr_layer_tabset, actionButton ptr_update_plot. ptr_outputs_panel (564-570): plot card + inline error + show-code toggle + draggable .ptr-code-window. ptr_make_app_server (302-354): shared reactives keyed by canonical_shared_id, ptr_server_internal(auto_bind_shared=TRUE), ptr_bind_local_shared_consumers(host_owned_keys=character(0)).
findings.verification: R/paintr-app.R ptr_build_app_ui (762-805): fluidPage(ptr_assets(css), div class='ptr-app ptr-app--page' > [ptr_ui_header, sidebarLayout(sidebarPanel(controls), mainPanel(outputs))]); embedded path (app_chrome=FALSE) returns tagList with no header/--page. ptr_controls_panel (411-449): shared_section_tags + ptr_layer_select_ui + ptr_layer_tabset_ui + ptr_update_button. ptr_outputs_panel renders plot/inline-error/code. ptr_make_app_server (302-354): builds shared_reactives keyed by canonical_shared_id, calls ptr_server_internal(auto_bind_shared=TRUE), then ptr_bind_local_shared_consumers(host_owned_keys = partition_host_owned_keys(new_partition_view('single'))) — the single-surface partition yields character(0), confirming single-instance owns every shared key with no coordinator. Substance holds; checked_refs were mangled (cited app.R:169/339 + partition-view.R) — corrected.

findings.claim: The seven pp* keywords split into exactly three placeholder roles — value (ppText/ppNum/ppExpr), data-consumer (ppVar), data-source (ppUpload) — plus two structural keywords (ppLayerOff/ppVerbSwitch) that are NOT placeholders.
findings.detail: ptr_register_builtins (R/paintr-builtins.R:310-393) wires each keyword via a role-specific define helper. These map onto three node constructors (R/paintr-nodes.R): ptr_ph_value (64-72), ptr_ph_data_consumer (74-83, carries `upstream` slot), ptr_ph_data_source (85-94, carries `shortcut_id` slot). ppLayerOff/ppVerbSwitch register as role='structural' (builtins 380-381); detect_placeholder returns NULL for them (translate.R:697) so they never become ptr_ph_* nodes — the translator special-unwraps them. build_placeholder_node (translate.R:1042-1059) dispatches on value/consumer/source only.
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-builtins.R ptr_register_builtins (define helpers at 332/342/349/356/367), R/paintr-nodes.R (ptr_ph_value 64, ptr_ph_data_consumer 74 with upstream slot, ptr_ph_data_source 85 with shortcut_id slot), R/paintr-translate.R detect_placeholder (720) returns NULL for role=='structural', build_placeholder_node (1075) dispatches on the three placeholder roles. ppLayerOff/ppVerbSwitch registered via ptr_register_structural_keyword (builtins 380-381), role='structural'. Three placeholder roles + two structural keywords contract unchanged by ADR-0027 (which only renamed public define args).

findings.claim: Each value/consumer/source keyword binds to a fixed Shiny widget and resolve function: ppText→textInput (strips one matched quote-pair), ppNum→numericInput (NA→drops arg), ppExpr→textAreaInput (parses to a language object), ppVar→shinyWidgets::pickerInput (multiple=TRUE+maxOptions=1L trick), ppUpload→fileInput (accept .csv/.tsv/.rds/.xlsx/.xls/.json).
findings.detail: From R/paintr-builtins.R: text→textInput (34) + strip_matched_quote_pair (51-59); num→numericInput (72), resolve NULL on non-numeric/NA so substitute marks arg missing+prune deletes it (104-111); expr→textAreaInput (129), resolve parses via rlang::parse_exprs requiring exactly ONE expression, signals ptr_partial_input on empty/mid-typing/parse-fail (139-172); var→shinyWidgets::pickerInput multiple=TRUE + pickerOptions(maxOptions=1L) legacy trick (no first-choice default, value starts character(0), click-to-deselect), resolve returns rlang::sym(value) (205-210); upload→shiny::fileInput accept=ptr_upload_accept_formats() (six formats), resolve_data→ptr_read_uploaded_data (249-252).
findings.verification: R/paintr-builtins.R: text->textInput, text_resolve strip_matched_quote_pair (51-59); num->numericInput, num_resolve NULL on non-numeric/NA (104-111); expr->textAreaInput, expr_resolve parses via rlang::parse_exprs requiring exactly ONE, ptr_signal_partial on empty/parse-fail (139-172); var->shinyWidgets::pickerInput multiple=TRUE + pickerOptions(maxOptions=1L) legacy trick, var_resolve rlang::sym(value) (176-210); upload->shiny::fileInput accept=ptr_upload_accept_formats() = c('.csv','.tsv','.rds','.xlsx','.xls','.json'), resolve_data->ptr_read_uploaded_data (225-252). All six accepted formats and every widget/resolve pairing match.

findings.claim: A pp* placeholder's single positional argument is its boot DEFAULT, validated UNEVALUATED by a per-keyword arg-validator (ppText=string-literal, ppNum=numeric-literal, ppVar/ppUpload=bareword-or-string, ppExpr=any-expr-verbatim); more than one positional arg, or a positional on a no-default keyword, aborts at translate time. The validator receives the unevaluated language object — the placeholder arg is NEVER eval()'d at translate time.
findings.detail: extract_placeholder_args (R/paintr-translate.R:903-957) splits named vs positional (912-914); at most one positional (>1 aborts 925-930) passed through entry$default_arg (931); positional with no default_arg aborts (918-924). Validators in R/paintr-default-args.R: ptr_arg_string (217-224) requires string literal; ptr_arg_numeric (228-236) constant-folds to length-1 non-NA numeric; ptr_arg_symbol_or_string (201-213) accepts bareword OR string; ptr_arg_expression (268-293) stores verbatim (warns if wrapped in quote/bquote/expr/quo). So ppText("hi"), ppNum(5), ppVar(mpg), ppExpr(factor(cyl)) all seed an initial widget value. Header comment 901-902 states no eval at translate time. Wired via positional_arg= in ptr_register_builtins (332-374).
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-translate.R extract_placeholder_args (936): splits named vs positional, at most one positional, passes through entry$default_arg (INTERNAL key, unchanged), positional with no default_arg aborts. Validators in R/paintr-default-args.R: ptr_arg_string (254), ptr_arg_numeric (288), ptr_arg_symbol_or_string (232), ptr_arg_expression (322) store/validate the unevaluated AST. Built-in wiring still seeds ppText/ppNum(5)/ppVar(mpg)/ppExpr(factor(cyl)). The internal node-record key is entry$default_arg (unchanged); only the PUBLIC constructor arg was renamed positional_arg->parse_positional_arg, which this claim does not name. Claim holds.

findings.claim: Any placeholder may take a named `shared="key"` string argument (the only named arg every keyword accepts) joining it to a shared coordinator; the value is read as a literal AST string (not eval'd), and one shared key may back only ONE role at a time.
findings.detail: extract_placeholder_args treats `shared` as always-recognized alongside declared named_args (translate.R:936); unknown named args abort (937-944). extract_shared_value (962-971) requires a single non-empty character literal in the AST. ptr_validate_shared_roles (1066-1086) buckets placeholders by $shared key and aborts if the same key is used by placeholders of different roles ('A shared widget can only back one role at a time'). So shared= is a cross-formula/cross-panel coordination handle valid on value, consumer, and source placeholders alike.
findings.verification: R/paintr-translate.R extract_placeholder_args: recognized = c('shared', declared named_args) (936); unknown named args abort (938-944); shared parsed by extract_shared_value (962-971) requiring a single non-empty character literal in the AST (not eval'd). ptr_validate_shared_roles (1066-1086) buckets placeholders by $shared key via collect_shared_occurrences and aborts when one key spans incompatible roles ('A shared widget can only back one role at a time'). shared= valid on value/consumer/source alike. Holds.

findings.claim: ppUpload with shortcut=TRUE auto-emits a sibling textInput at node$id+'_shortcut' for typing a dataset name, and a bareword positional (ppUpload(penguins)) seeds that shortcut as an env-load default — meaning load get('penguins') from the app env, not an inert token.
findings.detail: ppUpload registered shortcut=TRUE, positional_arg=ptr_arg_symbol_or_string (builtins:367-374). At id-assignment (ids.R:83-86) each source node with entry$shortcut TRUE gets node$shortcut_id<-paste0(node$id,'_shortcut'). The hook returns ONLY the fileInput (builtins:236-247); the shortcut textInput is STATIC UI from build_ui_for.ptr_ph_data_source (build-ui.R:96-108, value=node$default %||% '', label default 'Optional dataset name') so it survives the source uiOutput re-render. Outside ptr_app, ppUpload(penguins) is identity; no-arg ppUpload() aborts ('only meaningful inside ptr_app()'). Source registry shortcut contract: registry.R:735-784.
findings.verification: ppUpload registered shortcut=TRUE, positional_arg=ptr_arg_symbol_or_string() (builtins.R 367-373). R/paintr-ids.R assigns node$shortcut_id <- paste0(node$id, '_shortcut') (line 83). The upload hook ptr_builtin_upload_build_ui returns ONLY the fileInput (236-247); the shortcut textInput is STATIC UI from build_ui_for.ptr_ph_data_source in R/paintr-build-ui.R (def line 80, static-shortcut comment line 96). Runtime: ppUpload(penguins) is identity outside ptr_app; no-arg ppUpload() aborts ('only meaningful inside ptr_app()', builtins.R 413-418). Bareword positional seeds the shortcut default (env-load). Holds; ids.R line ~83 and build-ui.R ~80-108 confirmed.

findings.claim: ppExpr is the one value placeholder whose resolved value is wrapped in ptr_user_expr (not ptr_literal), so the parsed expression is spliced into the formula as live code rather than a literal value.
findings.detail: substitute_walk.ptr_ph_value (R/paintr-substitute.R:122-128) runs entry$resolve_expr then: if node$keyword=='ppExpr' returns ptr_user_expr(resolved); else ptr_literal(resolved). The expr resolver (builtins 139-172) parses textarea text via rlang::parse_exprs requiring exactly one, so the user typing factor(cyl) gets the AST factor(cyl) (not the string) substituted into the layer call. ptr_user_expr is its own typed-tree node (nodes.R:96-98) with predicate is_ptr_user_expr (nodes.R:129).
findings.verification: R/paintr-substitute.R substitute_walk.ptr_ph_value (line 122): runs the resolver then, if node$keyword=='ppExpr' (line 126), returns ptr_user_expr(resolved); else ptr_literal. expr resolver (builtins.R 139-172) parses textarea text via rlang::parse_exprs requiring exactly one -> the AST, not the string. ptr_user_expr is its own node (nodes.R:96-98) with predicate is_ptr_user_expr (nodes.R:129). Holds.

findings.claim: ppLayerOff(layer, hide=) and ppVerbSwitch(.data, verb, switch_on=, label=) are structural toggles unwrapped at translate time into boot-state metadata on a layer/stage node; their logical arg MUST be a literal so the formula stays the single source of truth, and they degrade to naked-R semantics outside ptr_app.
findings.detail: ppLayerOff marks a layer off-by-default: in ptr_app it becomes a ptr_layer with default_active=!hide + checkbox; outside ptr_app hide=TRUE returns NULL, hide=FALSE returns the layer (builtins 460-467). ppVerbSwitch makes a pipeline stage toggleable with positive sense (switch_on=TRUE applies the verb); inserts .data as the FIRST positional arg of verb_expr, takes optional label, validates the verb call shape (is.call) even when switch_on=FALSE; outside ptr_app switch_on=FALSE returns .data unchanged (builtins 526-550). The translator validates hide/switch_on as a length-1 non-NA logical LITERAL and aborts (class ptr_translate_error) on a variable/non-literal (translate.R:746-767, 870-894). default_stage_enabled/has_user_control/stage_label slots carry this metadata on ptr_call (nodes.R:32-57).
findings.verification: All cited symbols intact at HEAD 29571ac. ptr_register_structural_keyword present (R/paintr-registry.R:924). detect_placeholder returns NULL for role=='structural' (R/paintr-translate.R:730). validate_pp_off_hide (R/paintr-translate.R:779-800) still requires `hide` to be a length-1 non-NA logical literal and aborts otherwise with the 'single source of truth' message. ppVerbSwitch first-arg-insertion + structural unwrap path (is_structural_keyword_call:766, unwrap_pp_verb_switch_stage invoked at 204). ptr_call carries default_stage_enabled/has_user_control/stage_label (R/paintr-nodes.R:48-49) and default_active (15). The stale flag came only from registry.R being touched by the ADR-0027 rename (validate_input->validate_session_input, positional_arg->parse_positional_arg); that rename does NOT touch structural-keyword translation, node slots, or the literal-logical validation. Claim holds verbatim; line refs drifted (registry.R structural-kw 924; translate validators 779/204).

findings.claim: A user defines a custom placeholder by calling one of three public constructors keyed by ROLE — ptr_define_placeholder_value (self-contained widgets), ptr_define_placeholder_consumer (widgets needing upstream column names), ptr_define_placeholder_source (widgets that PRODUCE the data the rest of the formula reads) — all thin wrappers over the shared internal ptr_define_core(role, ...).
findings.detail: R/paintr-registry.R: the three @export'd fns (defs 592/713/870) each fix a `role` string and forward to ptr_define_core (352). Role drives: build_ui arity (consumer args c('node','cols','data'); value+source = 'node', 361-366); which resolve hook is required (source needs resolve_data + optional resolve_expr defaulting to function(value,node,...) rlang::sym(value); value/consumer need resolve_expr + optional validate_input); the shortcut flag (source only); data_aware = role %in% c('consumer','source') (line 408). Built-in exemplars: ppText/ppNum/ppExpr (value), ppVar (consumer), ppUpload (source). Pick value unless the widget needs upstream columns (→consumer) or itself yields the dataset (→source).
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-registry.R: three @export'd constructors ptr_define_placeholder_value (598), ptr_define_placeholder_consumer (721), ptr_define_placeholder_source — all forward role + args to ptr_define_core (355). Role still drives build_ui arity (consumer=c('node','cols','data') else 'node', 361-366), required resolve hook (source->resolve_data + resolve_expr defaulting to function(value,node,...) rlang::sym(value); value/consumer->resolve_expr), shortcut flag (source only), data_aware = role %in% c('consumer','source'). NAMESPACE still exports all three. ADR-0027 renamed only the inner arg NAMES, not these constructor names or the role split. Claim holds.

findings.claim: The placeholder registry is process-global (no `placeholders=` argument anywhere) and is deliberately anchored in options('.ggpaintr_registry_v1') rather than a plain package-namespace env so that repeated pkgload::load_all reloads (as devtools::test does) converge register and lookup onto the same session env instead of splitting across reload cycles.
findings.detail: R/paintr-registry.R:14-21 — .ptr_registry<-local({env<-getOption('.ggpaintr_registry_v1'); if not env, new.env(parent=emptyenv())+options()}). Header comment 5-13 explains the split-registry hazard: each load_all re-evaluates the top-level expr producing a NEW namespace env; anchoring in options() makes every cycle rebind the same env. ptr_define_core calls ensure_registry_initialized then ptr_registry_register which assign()s the entry into the global env (427). No per-app/per-call registry handle — defining a custom placeholder mutates global process state.
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-registry.R top (.ptr_registry anchored via getOption('.ggpaintr_registry_v1') else new.env(parent=emptyenv())+options(), with split-registry header comment), ensure_registry_initialized (33), ptr_registry_register assigning the entry into the global env (~427), and ptr_define_core. No `placeholders=` argument anywhere. Process-global, options-anchored design unchanged by ADR-0027. Claim holds.

findings.claim: A custom placeholder's hooks have role-specific required signatures validated at definition time: build_ui is function(node, ...) for value/source and function(node, cols, data, ...) for consumer; value/consumer supply resolve_expr(value, node, ...) (+ optional validate_session_input(value, ctx)); source supplies resolve_data(value, node, ...) (+ optional resolve_expr defaulting to rlang::sym(value)).
findings.detail: ptr_define_core wires validate_hook with required-arg lists: build_ui_args=c('node','cols','data') for consumer else 'node' (registry.R:361-366); source requires resolve_data(value,node) and defaults resolve_expr to function(value,node,...) rlang::sym(value) when NULL (368-374); non-source requires resolve_expr(value,node) and optional validate_input(value,ctx) (382-385). validate_hook (156-180) is lenient: a hook declaring ONLY `...` passes (warns if required args were named); any hook with `...` passes; a hook missing a required named arg AND lacking `...` aborts. resolve_expr return contract (docblock 506-513): scalar atomic / rlang::sym / a call / NULL to PRUNE the argument. build_ui docblock: end signature with `...`, pass node$id as widget inputId.
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-registry.R ptr_define_core (355) and validate_hook (156). build_ui_args = c('node','cols','data') for consumer else 'node'. Source requires resolve_data(value,node) and defaults resolve_expr to function(value,node,...) rlang::sym(value) when NULL; non-source requires resolve_expr(value,node). validate_hook is lenient: a hook declaring only `...` passes (warns if required named args), any hook with `...` passes, a hook missing a required named arg AND lacking `...` aborts. The OPTIONAL validation hook is now named validate_session_input in the public arg / validate_hook call (R/paintr-registry.R:386-387) — the claim names it 'validate_input', the OLD public arg name (ADR-0027 renamed validate_input->validate_session_input). Internal record key stays entry$validate_input. Rewriting to use validate_session_input for the public hook name.

findings.claim: The ptr_arg_* helper factories (ptr_arg_symbol_or_string/ptr_arg_string/ptr_arg_symbol/ptr_arg_numeric — each taking a `vector=` flag; ptr_arg_numeric also a `length=` check in vector mode — plus ptr_arg_expression) return a closure function(arg_expr)->canonical|abort that validates a placeholder's default ARGUMENT (unevaluated AST) without calling eval/parse on arbitrary user input; ptr_arg_expression stores its input verbatim (warns only if wrapped in quote/bquote/rlang::expr/rlang::quo). They are wired into a placeholder via parse_positional_arg=/parse_named_args= (renamed from positional_arg=/named_args= by ADR-0027); a NULL parse_positional_arg (the default) REJECTS positional args at translate time. (ptr_arg_numeric_vector() was removed; use ptr_arg_numeric(vector=TRUE).)
findings.detail: R/paintr-default-args.R header (8-13): validators never eval/parse/deparse-reparse. ptr_arg_symbol_or_string (201) returns rlang::as_string for a symbol or the string; ptr_arg_string (217) accepts only a string literal; ptr_arg_numeric (228) and ptr_arg_numeric_vector (240) call ptr_constant_fold (192) which validates the AST via validate_constant_ast (149) against an allowlist env and rlang::eval_bare()s in a sealed env — so 2*pi / seq_len(3) admitted, arbitrary calls abort; numeric requires length-1 non-NA, numeric_vector allows a vector + optional length check. ptr_arg_expression (268) returns input UNCHANGED and warns once on quote/bquote/expr/quo wrappers (.ptr_expr_wrapper_names 264; warn 283-289). Built-in wiring: ppNum positional_arg=ptr_arg_numeric() (builtins 343-346), ppExpr positional_arg=ptr_arg_expression() (350-353). Registry docblock 520-527: NULL positional_arg rejects positional args.
findings.verification: Re-verified @ HEAD: trigger was the unrelated paintr-translate.R comment swap. The five ptr_arg_* factories + vector= flag + AST-only constant fold are unchanged in R/paintr-default-args.R. Claim holds.

findings.claim: Two non-obvious registration details: (1) a source placeholder with shortcut=TRUE gets the framework to auto-render a sibling textInput at node$shortcut_id (the ADR-0024/0025 env-name data-loader / spec round-trip carrier) — the author's build_ui must render ONLY node$id or the id binds twice; (2) every define_* call accepts ui_text_defaults (named list over label/help/placeholder/empty_text with {param} interpolation), stored on the registry entry under the internal key copy_defaults.
findings.detail: R/paintr-registry.R: source build_ui docblock (739-746) — with shortcut=TRUE the framework emits the sibling shortcut textInput; author renders only the payload widget (or NULL for shortcut-only). The shortcut value (typed dataset name) round-trips through spec= and is looked up via get(name, envir, inherits=TRUE) as the source frame (ADR-0024 @section, 823-839; inherits=TRUE at 832). validate_copy_defaults (311-337) restricts names to ptr_ui_text_leaf_fields()=label/help/placeholder/empty_text (copy.R:164-166), single non-NA strings; ptr_define_core stores as entry$copy_defaults (413) though the public arg is ui_text_defaults (note 348-351). named_args may not contain 'shared' (reserved; validate_named_args 247-253). ptr_clear_placeholder (69-111) refuses the protected built-in set (ppText/ppNum/ppExpr/ppVar/ppUpload PLUS structural ppLayerOff/ppVerbSwitch).
findings.verification: Both details hold at HEAD. (1) Source shortcut companion: shortcut=TRUE round-trips the typed dataset name through spec= and the env-load lookup get(lookup_name, envir=state$eval_env, inherits=TRUE) is present in R/paintr-server.R (lines moved to 1243 and 1492 by the unrelated safe_to_remove threading fix, but the inherits=TRUE env-load contract is unchanged). (2) ui_text_defaults: validate_copy_defaults (R/paintr-registry.R:311) restricts names to ptr_ui_text_leaf_fields() = c('label','help','placeholder','empty_text') (R/paintr-copy.R:164-166), requires single non-NA strings, and ptr_define_core stores under the INTERNAL key entry$copy_defaults (R/paintr-registry.R:416) while the PUBLIC arg stays ui_text_defaults (359) — explicitly documented at 307-310. named_args reserves 'shared' (validate_named_args R/paintr-registry.R:247). ptr_clear_placeholder (69) refuses the protected set via ptr_builtin_keywords() (R/paintr-builtins.R:259). ADR-0027 did NOT rename ui_text_defaults or copy_defaults. The stale flag came from server.R being touched; the cited env-load contract survived. Claim holds.

findings.claim: Each ptr_define_placeholder_* call RETURNS a runtime callable (and registers as a side effect) so the author can bind it under the keyword name — `ppPct <- ptr_define_placeholder_value('ppPct', ...)` — giving the keyword a plain-R meaning outside ptr_app(); the default for value/consumer is embellish_identity() (the identity function(x,...)x) and for source an abort-guard, overridable via the embellish_eval= argument (renamed from runtime= by ADR-0027; the internal record key stays entry$runtime).
findings.detail: R/paintr-registry.R: ptr_define_core builds runtime_default (393-402) = identity for value/consumer (401), or for source a closure aborting '`<kw>()` is only meaningful inside `ptr_app()`.' (396-398); runtime_fn<-runtime %||% runtime_default and the function RETURNS runtime_fn (420). Docblocks (550-563/674-682/798-809) state the return is bound under the same identifier as the keyword. ptr_check_keyword_lhs_drift (282-304) is a best-effort tripwire (R's `<-` usually pushes no call frame so a plain top-level binding does not trigger it). validate_keyword (142-154) rejects reserved/non-syntactic names; validate_keyword_no_shadow (201-216) rejects keywords shadowing base/stats/utils/methods/graphics/grDevices/ggplot2 (the pp-prefix convention sidesteps this).
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-registry.R ptr_define_core (355). runtime_default is built (396-405): for source a closure aborting '`<kw>()` is only meaningful inside `ptr_app()`.', for value/consumer it is now embellish_identity() (NEW exported helper in R/paintr-embellish.R:40, returns the identity function(x,...)x). runtime_fn <- embellish_eval %||% runtime_default (406) and the function RETURNS runtime_fn (423). The PUBLIC arg is now embellish_eval (renamed from runtime= by ADR-0027); the INTERNAL record key stays entry$runtime (415). ptr_check_keyword_lhs_drift (282) tripwire and validate_keyword/validate_keyword_no_shadow guards still present. The claim's phrasing 'default runtime is identity function(x,...)x' is correct in effect but the value/consumer default is now produced by embellish_identity(); rewriting to name the current default source and the embellish_eval override arg.

findings.claim: The ptr_server server-side area exposes TWO exported entry points, not one: ptr_server (the single public layout/render server, @export at R/paintr-app.R:1091) AND ptr_init_state (also @export'd, NAMESPACE:106); only ptr_server_internal is unexported @noRd plumbing.
findings.detail: R/paintr-app.R:1091 is `#' @export` above ptr_server (1092); roxygen 1006-1008 says 'The single public server-side entry point ... used identically at L2 ... and L3'; 1019-1021 confirms no user-authored moduleServer/lower-level public server. ptr_server_internal (R/paintr-server.R:801) carries @keywords internal + @noRd and is absent from NAMESPACE. But ptr_init_state (def server.R:84) is preceded by @export at line 83 and appears as export(ptr_init_state) at NAMESPACE:106 — the prior 'unexported plumbing' claim about it is FALSE.
findings.verification: Two exported server-side entry points confirmed. ptr_server: R/paintr-app.R roxygen '#The **single public server-side entry point**' (1006), @export above the def at 1091-1092; NAMESPACE:115 export(ptr_server). ptr_init_state: R/paintr-server.R @export precedes def at 90-91; NAMESPACE:106 export(ptr_init_state). ptr_server_internal: R/paintr-server.R carries @keywords internal + @noRd (806-807) and is NOT in NAMESPACE. findings.claim: At L2 the user owns the entire shinyApp shell: they write their own fluidPage + server function and merely drop ptr_ui(formula,id) into the page and ptr_server(formula,id) into the server with matching id; ggpaintr does not call shinyApp (that is L1's ptr_app). ptr_server self-namespaces via shiny::moduleServer(id,...), so the user writes no moduleServer.
findings.detail: Roxygen @examples for ptr_server (R/paintr-app.R:1069-1077): ui=fluidPage(ptr_ui(f,'p')), server=function(...){ptr_server(f,'p')} — user-authored shinyApp(). @param id (1047-1049) says id 'must match the id passed to ptr_ui()' — it is the namespace prefix. ptr_server wraps its body in shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input,output,session){...}) at 1166. Repo fixture module-app/app.R:10-18 confirms: ui=fluidPage(titlePanel('My host app'), ptr_ui(formula)); server=function{ptr_server(formula)}; user's own shinyApp(ui,server). Non-ggpaintr chrome (titlePanel, other widgets, custom layout) lives in the user's code, not ggpaintr's — the structural difference from L1.
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-app.R: ptr_server (def line 1128) wraps its body in shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input,output,session){...}) at line 1206; @param id (947-949) states it must match the id passed to the piece functions and is the namespace prefix; roxygen @examples (1104-1126) show user-authored shiny::shinyApp(ui=fluidPage(ptr_ui(!!f,'p')), server=function(...){ptr_server(!!f,'p')}). Fixture tests/testthat/fixtures/vignette-apps/module-app/app.R:10-18 confirms ui=fluidPage(titlePanel('My host app'), ptr_ui(formula)); server=function{ptr_server(formula)}; user's own shinyApp(ui,server). Claim holds verbatim; only the cited line numbers drifted (moduleServer 1166->1206 etc.), behavior unchanged.

findings.claim: ptr_server() calls shiny::moduleServer(id,...) internally and must be called BARE inside the user's server function — wrapping it in the user's own shiny::moduleServer would double-namespace (ADR-0006: one public server, no user moduleServer). The id arg IS the namespace, passed positionally as the 2nd arg; id=NULL → moduleServer(NULL) → NS(NULL) identity.
findings.detail: R/paintr-app.R:1166 opens shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input,output,session){...}) inside ptr_server (def 1092-1093), and the block's last expression is `state` (1228), so ptr_server IS the module server, not nested under one. The module-app fixture server body is simply `ptr_server(formula)` (no user moduleServer). e2e comment (test-e2e:180-182) states id=NULL→NS(NULL) identity; module-app omits id→bare ids, l2-shared uses id→prefixed ids plot_1-ptr_plot.

The id arg is the 2nd positional arg and is the namespace; id=NULL -> moduleServer(NULL) -> NS(NULL) identity (consistent with @param id at 1047-1049 'identity namespace'). findings.claim: ptr_server returns the ptr_state object (class c('ptr_state','list')) as its internal moduleServer block's value — the L2→L3 bridge: an L2 user can ignore the return for a turn-key plot, while an L3 user captures it and drives a custom render path. Its supported L3 handle is the reactive state$runtime() whose result carries $plot/$code/$error; server_ns_fn/ui_ns_fn are internal plumbing, NOT a public escape hatch.

findings.claim: ptr_ui_page(..., page=shiny::fluidPage, css=NULL) is the L3 page-shell escape hatch: page(ptr_assets(css), tags$div(class='ptr-app', ...)). page must be a BS3 page builder (fluidPage/fixedPage/fillPage/bootstrapPage/basicPage) — NOT navbarPage (needs positional title) and NOT bslib/BS5 roots (bundled CSS is BS3-scoped). ptr_ui_controls is the bare controls tagList with NO .ptr-app wrapper and NO assets, so it must be rendered inside ptr_ui_page or equivalent.

Default page arg is the function shiny::fluidPage.

findings.claim: Every L3 ptr_ui_* output piece is a namespaced Shiny output bound to a fixed, server-owned id (plot→ptr_plot, error→ptr_error, code→ptr_code), so an L3 layout works only when each piece's id= matches the id= passed to ptr_server(); ptr_server registers all three regardless of which pieces the UI places, so an unplaced piece is a harmless no-op and a mismatched id silently produces a blank output.
findings.detail: ptr_ui_plot(id)→plot_card_tag(NS(id)) emitting plotOutput(ns('ptr_plot')) (R/paintr-app.R:479,598-600); ptr_ui_error(id)→uiOutput(ns('ptr_error')) (456-458,618-620); ptr_ui_code(id)→verbatimTextOutput(ns('ptr_code')) (543,556,644-646). These ids are the contract ptr_register_plot/_error/_code write to: server.R ptr_register_plot writes state$server_ns_fn('ptr_plot') (3504), _error 'ptr_error' (3527), _code 'ptr_code' (3584). ptr_server registers all three (860-862). Pieces carry NO server coupling beyond the shared id+namespace.
findings.verification: Output ids are server-owned constants. ptr_ui_plot(id) -> plot_card_tag(NS(id)) emitting plotOutput(ns('ptr_plot')) (R/paintr-app.R:479, 598-599); ptr_ui_error(id) -> uiOutput(ns('ptr_error')) (457, 618-619); ptr_ui_code(id) -> verbatimTextOutput(ns('ptr_code')) (543/556, 644-645). These match what the register family writes: R/paintr-server.R ptr_register_plot writes output[[state$server_ns_fn('ptr_plot')]] (3525), ptr_register_error 'ptr_error' (3548), ptr_register_code 'ptr_code' (3605). ptr_setup_runtime calls all three unconditionally (849, 867-869) regardless of which UI pieces are placed, so an unplaced piece is a no-op and a mismatched id yields a blank output. Claim holds; line numbers drifted.

findings.claim: The single-piece ptr_ui_* builders (plot/error/code/controls/header) emit NO CSS/JS assets; assets are a separate mandatory piece — public ptr_ui_assets() / internal ptr_assets() — that an L3 hand-composed page must include exactly once (normally via ptr_ui_page()). ptr_ui_assets is a tagList of two htmlDependency objects (structural-layer + cosmetic ggpaintr.css/JS) plus optional user <link>s, so emitting it any number of times collapses to one <head> injection of each dependency.
findings.detail: ptr_ui_assets(css)→ptr_assets(css) (R/paintr-build-ui.R:805-807) = shiny::tagList(ptr_layer_assets() [htmlDependency 'ggpaintr-layer': ggpaintr-layer.css + ptr_set_class handler], core_assets_dep() [htmlDependency 'ggpaintr': ggpaintr.css + code-window JS ggpaintr-ui.js], ptr_user_css_assets(css)) (774-780). User <link>s emitted AFTER ggpaintr.css (3rd element) so equal-specificity user rules win. htmltools dedupes each htmlDependency to one head injection (comment 752-768, replaced an old window.__ptr_ui_assets_registered guard). Single-piece-builders-emit-no-assets per ptr_ui_assets roxygen 790-794.
findings.verification: R/paintr-build-ui.R: ptr_ui_assets(css) -> ptr_assets(css) (805-806); ptr_assets(css) (774-779) = shiny::tagList(ptr_layer_assets(), core_assets_dep(), ptr_user_css_assets(css)). ptr_layer_assets() (672-678) = htmlDependency name 'ggpaintr-layer' with ggpaintr-layer.css + ggpaintr-layer.js. core_assets_dep() (693-707) = htmlDependency name 'ggpaintr' with ggpaintr.css + ggpaintr-ui.js (and still calls addResourcePath('ggpaintr', www) at 700 for the header logo). ptr_user_css_assets(css) (719) emits user <link>s AFTER ggpaintr.css (comment 758-759) so equal-specificity user rules win. Comment 761 confirms components 1 & 2 are htmlDependency objects so htmltools dedupes each to one head injection (the old window.__ptr_ui_assets_registered JS guard at 692 is referenced as replaced). Single-piece builders emit no assets. Claim holds; line numbers drifted.

findings.claim: core_assets_dep() still calls shiny::addResourcePath('ggpaintr', www) IN ADDITION to the htmlDependency, because htmlDependency does NOT register the legacy 'ggpaintr/...' resource prefix that ptr_ui_header()'s absolute-path logo ref depends on.
findings.detail: R/paintr-build-ui.R:693-710. www=system.file('www',package='ggpaintr'); when nzchar(www) it calls shiny::addResourcePath('ggpaintr', www) (699-701) AND returns htmlDependency(name='ggpaintr', stylesheet='ggpaintr.css', script='ggpaintr-ui.js') (702-709). Comment 695-698: the CSS/JS ride as a deduped htmlDependency, but ptr_ui_header() references the logo by legacy absolute path 'ggpaintr/...', so the whole www dir is also served under that prefix. Deliberate belt-and-suspenders; matches the htmldependency-resource-prefix project memory.
findings.verification: www=system.file; addResourcePath('ggpaintr',www) at 699-701 + htmlDependency at 702-709; comment 695-698 confirms logo legacy-path rationale.

findings.claim: ptr_ui_inline_error and ptr_ui_toggle_code are pure DOM combinators (no server wiring) that mutate an already-built plot piece's children to add behaviour, with a structural fallback that degrades to siblings when handed an arbitrary custom output instead of a ggpaintr card. The </> toggle is purely client-side JS scoped to the nearest .ptr-output ancestor — no Shiny input/output.
findings.detail: ptr_ui_inline_error(plot,error) appends the error slot to plot$children[[2]] for the designed card; non-card → tagList(plot,error) (R/paintr-app.R:671-682). ptr_ui_toggle_code(plotish,code) injects the </> code_toggle_button into the card head (plotish$children[[1]]) and wraps in one .ptr-output div; non-card (shiny.tag.list like plotly/ggiraph, or childless tag) → emits the toggle as a sibling inside .ptr-output with no $children mutation (723-756). Design intent: behaviour added compositionally by combinators, NOT by flags on ptr_ui_plot, which is 'truly bare' (581-584).
findings.verification: R/paintr-app.R: ptr_ui_inline_error(plot, error) (671-682) appends the error slot to plot$children[[2]] (the card body) when plot is a shiny.tag with >=2 children; the non-card fallback returns shiny::tagList(plot, error). ptr_ui_toggle_code(plotish, code) (723-756) injects code_toggle_button() into plotish$children[[1]] (card head) and wraps in one .ptr-output div for the card path; the non-card path (shiny.tag.list or childless tag) emits the toggle button as an explicit sibling inside .ptr-output with no $children mutation. Both are pure DOM, no Shiny input/output; the </> toggle is client-side JS keyed off .ptr-code-toggle and the nearest .ptr-output ancestor (code_toggle_button 464-472; roxygen 692-693 'hides/shows that window purely DOM-locally'). ptr_ui_plot roxygen (581-584) states the piece is 'truly bare', behaviour added by combinators not flags. Claim holds; line numbers drifted.

findings.claim: ptr_ui_code(style=) defaults to 'panel' (a plain always-visible code card needing no wiring); style='window' produces a draggable slide-out window hidden until toggled and ONLY functions when wired through ptr_ui_toggle_code(). Both styles carry a code-mode toggle (radioGroupButtons ptr_code_mode, Final code=final / Spec=spec, default final) the server reads in ptr_register_code().
findings.detail: code_block_tag(ns,style): match.arg defaults 'panel'; 'window'→code_window_tag(verbatimTextOutput(ns('ptr_code')), mode_toggle=code_mode_toggle(ns)); 'panel'→.ptr-card--code with the same verbatimTextOutput (R/paintr-app.R:539-559). Window show/hide driven by bundled JS toggling .ptr-open on .ptr-code-window — no standalone show control, so bare style='window' without the toggle stays hidden (ptr_ui_code roxygen 632-636). code_mode_toggle (498-510): radioGroupButtons inputId ns('ptr_code_mode'), choices c(Final code=final, Spec=spec), selected 'final'; server reads input$ptr_code_mode in ptr_register_code (server.R:3585 mode_id<-server_ns_fn('ptr_code_mode')).
findings.verification: R/paintr-app.R code_block_tag(ns, style=c('panel','window')) (539-559): match.arg defaults 'panel'; 'window' -> code_window_tag(verbatimTextOutput(ns('ptr_code')), mode_toggle=code_mode_toggle(ns)); 'panel' -> .ptr-card--code with the same verbatimTextOutput. ptr_ui_code(id, style) (644-645) calls code_block_tag(NS(id), style). Window show/hide is bundled JS toggling .ptr-open on .ptr-code-window (code_window_tag 518-537); ptr_ui_code @param style roxygen (632-636) says style='window' is hidden until toggled and only works when wired via ptr_ui_toggle_code(). Both styles carry the code-mode toggle: code_mode_toggle(ns) (498-510) = radioGroupButtons inputId ns('ptr_code_mode'), choices c(`Final code`='final', Spec='spec'), selected 'final'. Server reads it: R/paintr-server.R ptr_register_code mode_id <- state$server_ns_fn('ptr_code_mode') (3606). Claim holds; line numbers drifted.

findings.claim: ptr_ui_controls is the generated-control bundle (layer pickerInput + hidden per-layer tabset + Update-plot button) as a bare tagList, and its shared= arg governs which shared= placeholders render inline: NULL (default) renders every shared key inline; a ptr_shared_spec excludes shared$panel_keys (those belong to the standalone ptr_shared_panel()).
findings.detail: ptr_ui_controls(formula,id,ui_text,expr_check,shared) asserts shared is NULL or ptr_shared_spec, ptr_translate()s, sets exclude_keys = if(is.null(shared)) character() else shared$panel_keys, builds inline shared section via shared_section_tags(...,exclude_keys=), then ptr_controls_panel(render_shared_section=FALSE) and tagLists via drop_null (R/paintr-app.R:951-966). ptr_controls_panel (401-438): pickerInput ns('ptr_layer_select'), hidden tabsetPanel ns('ptr_layer_tabset') type='hidden' of per-layer build_ui_for(layer) panels, actionButton ns('ptr_update_plot'). Carries NO .ptr-app wrapper and NO assets — must sit inside a .ptr-app Bootstrap page (use ptr_ui_page). shared_section_tags drops entries whose key is in exclude_keys (368-375).
findings.verification: Re-read ptr_ui_controls (R/paintr-app.R 981-1002): asserts shared is NULL or ptr_shared_spec, ptr_translate()s, sets exclude_keys = if(is.null(shared)) character() else shared$panel_keys, builds shared_section_tags(...,exclude_keys=), then ptr_controls_panel(render_shared_section=FALSE), combines via do.call(tagList, drop_null(...)). ptr_controls_panel def at line 401; shared_section_tags def at line 368. @param shared (958-964) documents NULL=>every shared key inline, ptr_shared_spec=>exclude shared$panel_keys (standalone ptr_shared_panel). Bare tagList, no .ptr-app wrapper / no assets. Claim holds; line numbers drifted (controls_panel 401-438, shared_section_tags 368).

findings.claim: ptr_ui_text is NOT an L3 UI-rendering piece despite the ptr_ui_ prefix — it is the validate-once copy-schema front door: handed a plain override list it validates+normalizes+deep-merges over the defaults and returns a classed `ptr_ui_text` object; handed an already-ptr_ui_text object it returns it unchanged, so the same merged object can be reused across ptr_app()/ptr_server() without re-validating.
findings.detail: R/paintr-copy.R:612-628: inherits(ui_text,'ptr_ui_text') short-circuits (613-615); NULL→ptr_default_ui_text() (617-620); else ptr_validate_ui_text() (strict: aborts on unknown section/shell entry/keyword/leaf) + ptr_normalize_ui_text() + ptr_deep_merge_ui_text over unclass(defaults), reclassed 'ptr_ui_text'. The class IS the validated-once marker. Defaults assembled at call time from the live placeholder registry via ptr_registry_keywords()/ptr_registry_lookup() copy_defaults (30-35). Override schema (roxygen 572-594): shell/upload/layer_checkbox/defaults/params/layers paths; leaf fields label/help/placeholder/empty_text with {param}/{layer} tokens; __unnamed__ for positional args. Lives in paintr-copy.R, not build-ui — UI builders merely accept its output as ui_text=. ptr_ui_header(title) (a static branded header bar) is adjacent but distinct.
findings.verification: Read ptr_ui_text in R/paintr-copy.R (~558-628): inherits(ui_text,'ptr_ui_text') short-circuits returning it unchanged; NULL -> ptr_default_ui_text(); else ptr_validate_ui_text() + ptr_normalize_ui_text() + ptr_deep_merge_ui_text over unclass(defaults), reclassed structure(...,class='ptr_ui_text'). Defaults assembled from the live placeholder registry via ptr_registry_keywords()/ptr_registry_lookup() copy_defaults (~28-63). Confirmed it is the validate-once copy-schema front door, not a UI-rendering piece, and that the class is the validated marker. ptr_ui_header(title) is the adjacent static header (~808). Claim holds; refs current.

findings.claim: ptr_resolve_ui_text() resolves a placeholder control's copy through a fixed `defaults → params → layers` specificity chain via two nested ptr_deep_merge_ui_text() calls, so a more-specific layer/param leaf field overrides the keyword default while unspecified fields fall through.
findings.detail: R/paintr-copy.R:749-773: for component='control' it looks up default_rule=rules$defaults[[keyword]], param_rule=rules$params[[param_key]][[keyword]], layer_rule=rules$layers[[layer_name]][[keyword]][[param_key]], then ptr_deep_merge_ui_text(ptr_deep_merge_ui_text(default_rule, param_rule), layer_rule) — later args win, field-by-field (deep merge 532-555). The param-specific override for alpha/ppNum (77-83 supplies label 'Transparency' + help 'Enter a value between 0 and 1.') layers over the ppNum default. Non-control components walk a fixed storage path from ptr_ui_text_component_paths() and abort if missing. After resolution every leaf field is token-interpolated for {param}/{layer} (778-784).
findings.verification: Control branch builds default/param/layer_rule and nests two deep merges (defaults→params→layers); component-map branch walks fixed paths + aborts on missing; leaf interpolation 778-784; alpha/ppNum override 77-83.

findings.claim: Copy-rule param keys are normalized before lookup: `colour` aliases to `color` and any empty/positional param collapses to the literal `__unnamed__` key, which is the only way to label a layer's positional argument (e.g. facet_wrap/facet_grid formulas). In ptr_resolve_ui_text the param_rule branch SKIPS __unnamed__ while the layer_rule branch allows it, so positional layer copy resolves only through the layers path.
findings.detail: ptr_ui_text_param_aliases() (copy.R:154-158) holds the single alias colour→color; ptr_normalize_param_key() (201-213) returns '__unnamed__' for unnamed params else applies the alias map. Normalization happens twice: at override-ingest in ptr_normalize_ui_text() (470-523, deep-merging colliding aliases) and again at resolve (750). The default copy tree registers facet_wrap/facet_grid positional copy under layers$<layer>$ppExpr$__unnamed__ (120-141). param_rule guarded by !identical(param_key,'__unnamed__') (752); layer_rule (762-768) has no such guard.
findings.verification: single alias colour→color; normalize_param_key returns __unnamed__ for unnamed; normalize_ui_text rekeys+deep-merges; default tree facet ppExpr$__unnamed__; param_rule skips __unnamed__ (752), layer_rule allows it (762-768).

findings.claim: ptr_options() is a base-options()-style getter/setter backed by a single-entry registry (ptr_settings) exposing exactly one knob, `verbose`, a logical scalar mapped to base option `ggpaintr.verbose` (default FALSE).
findings.detail: R/paintr-options.R:5-10 defines ptr_settings with only verbose→option 'ggpaintr.verbose' default FALSE. ptr_options() (47-87): no args returns all current settings as a named list; named args set via do.call(options,...) and invisibly return PREVIOUS values (enabling old<-ptr_options(verbose=TRUE); do.call(ptr_options,old) round-trip). Strict, registry-bound: args must be named, unknown names abort, ptr_validate_setting_value (95-115) requires every value to be a length-1 non-NA logical — no string/numeric/vector knob by design.
findings.verification: Read 1-116; single verbose knob, getter/setter returning prev invisibly, logical-scalar validator.

findings.claim: ptr_normalize_column_names() makes column names syntactic+unique+reserved-safe in a fixed order: punctuation→underscore, collapse/trim underscores, empty→'col', non-syntactic-start prefixed with 'X', make.unique, reserved-word suffix '_', then make.unique again.
findings.detail: R/paintr-data.R:77-107 (worker): coerce to char, NA→'', pad/truncate to ncol, gsub non-[:alnum:_.]+→'_', collapse '_+' and strip edge '_', empty→'col', prefix 'X' where name doesn't start with a letter or dot-not-followed-by-digit (regex (^[[:alpha:]])|(^\.(?![0-9]))), make.unique sep='_', append '_' to any of the 19 reserved words in .ptr_reserved_words (109-129), then make.unique AGAIN to break collisions the suffixing introduced. Public ptr_normalize_column_names() (31-33) calls ptr_normalize_tabular_data(source='Data').
findings.verification: Read 1-137; worker executes the exact claimed order; .ptr_reserved_words has 19 entries; public wrapper delegates.

findings.claim: Uploaded data is column-normalized automatically by the runtime on every successful ppUpload, but in-session data frames referenced by name from a formula are NOT — the user must pipe them through ptr_normalize_column_names() themselves before ptr_app(). The normalization rule is identical for both paths; only who-invokes-it differs.
findings.detail: R/paintr-data.R:1-33 roxygen states the contract: every successful ppUpload return passes through ptr_normalize_tabular_data (or its data.frame-coercing path for non-data.frame readRDS/readxl/jsonlite returns), so uploads are always safe; public ptr_normalize_column_names() exists for in-session frames with spaces/reserved-words/duplicates supplied by name. ptr_normalize_tabular_data() (42-68) preserves an existing data.frame subclass (renames in place) and coerces non-data.frame inputs via as.data.frame(), aborting with a source-labelled message on failure.
findings.verification: Roxygen 1-13 states auto-normalize on upload vs manual for in-session; normalize_tabular_data preserves subclass / coerces / aborts; both paths feed the same worker.

findings.claim: Constant folding lets a placeholder's numeric POSITIONAL DEFAULT be written as an arithmetic/sequence expression (e.g. ppNum(2*pi), ppNum(seq_len(3))) by AST-walking it against an allowlist and then evaluating it in a sealed env (new.env(parent=baseenv())) at build time, instead of a deparse-and-reparse or an open eval() of untrusted text - the same 'AST-only, no eval of arbitrary text' stance as the formula walker. The numeric validator is ptr_arg_numeric() (scalar by default, vector via ptr_arg_numeric(vector=TRUE); the old ptr_arg_numeric_vector() was removed); it calls ptr_constant_fold() -> validate_constant_ast() (recursive descent accepting syntactic literals, scalar atomics, allowlisted symbols inherits=TRUE, and calls whose head is an allowlisted symbol inherits=FALSE) -> rlang::eval_bare() in ptr_constant_fold_env (parent baseenv). The sealed env is the eval-safety mechanism; the allowlist + walker is the gate.
findings.detail: ptr_arg_numeric()/ptr_arg_numeric_vector() (R/paintr-default-args.R:228-261) call ptr_constant_fold() (192-195): validate_constant_ast() (149-190) recursively descends every node, accepting syntactic literals, already-evaluated scalar atomics, allowlisted symbols (exists inherits=TRUE so pi/Inf/NaN resolve via baseenv parent), and calls whose head is an allowlisted symbol (inherits=FALSE), then rlang::eval_bare()s in ptr_constant_fold_env whose parent is baseenv() and whose bindings are the registered names. The sealed env is the eval-safety mechanism; the allowlist + walker is the gate.
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-default-args.R. Mechanism fully intact: validate_constant_ast (166-207) recursively descends every node, accepting syntactic literals (167), already-evaluated scalar atomics (172), allowlisted symbols via exists(name,env,inherits=TRUE) so pi/Inf resolve through baseenv parent (174-183), and calls whose head is an allowlisted symbol via exists(name,env,inherits=FALSE) (184-194), recursing into each non-missing arg (196-201). ptr_constant_fold (209-212) calls validate then rlang::eval_bare(expr, ptr_constant_fold_env_get()); the env is new.env(parent=baseenv()) (line 81). The AST-only/no-eval-of-arbitrary-text stance is unchanged. ONE drift: the entry point ptr_arg_numeric_vector() was REMOVED (ADR 0027 / default-args rework); the numeric validator is now ptr_arg_numeric(vector=FALSE, length=NULL) (288), with vector mode via ptr_arg_numeric(vector=TRUE). ptr_arg_numeric (scalar branch line 298) calls ptr_constant_fold(arg_expr).

findings.claim: The constant-fold allowlist is seeded at package load with exactly arithmetic ops (- + * / ^ %% %/%) and sequence constructors (: c seq seq.int seq_len seq_along); literals (TRUE/FALSE/NA/NA_*_/Inf/NaN) and pi are recognized WITHOUT registration. Authors extend it via the exported ptr_register_constant_fold(name,value) / ptr_clear_constant_fold(name=NULL) / ptr_constant_fold_keywords(); clearing with NULL wipes-then-reseeds the built-ins, clearing a name removes just that entry.
findings.detail: ptr_register_constant_fold_builtins() (R/paintr-default-args.R:137-145) assigns arith=c('-','+','*','/','^','%%','%/%') and seq_ops=c(':','c','seq','seq.int','seq_len','seq_along') (13 names) from baseenv; called from .onLoad (builtins:552-554) and ptr_clear_constant_fold(NULL). Walker recognizes literals via is_syntactic_literal (150) and pi resolves through the env's baseenv parent (inherits=TRUE, 160). NAMESPACE: ptr_clear_constant_fold:95, ptr_constant_fold_keywords:97, ptr_register_constant_fold:113 — all exported. register asserts non-empty string + assign()s; clear NULL→rm-all then reseed builtins; named branch rm if exists inherits=FALSE; keywords()→sort(ls(all.names=TRUE)). Deliberately pure operators — no general math (log/sqrt) by default.
findings.verification: Read ptr_register_constant_fold_builtins (R/paintr-default-args.R ~79-145): arith=c('-','+','*','/','^','%%','%/%') and seq_ops=c(':','c','seq','seq.int','seq_len','seq_along') (13 names) registered from baseenv; literals via is_syntactic_literal and pi resolved through baseenv parent (inherits=TRUE). Builtins registered from .onLoad in R/paintr-builtins.R (~552-554) and re-seeded by ptr_clear_constant_fold(NULL). ptr_register_constant_fold asserts non-empty string then assign()s; clear(NULL) rm-all then reseed, named branch rm if exists. NAMESPACE exports ptr_clear_constant_fold, ptr_constant_fold_keywords, ptr_register_constant_fold (confirmed via grep). Claim holds.

findings.claim: All access to the fold env MUST go through ptr_constant_fold_env_get() (which get()s from asNamespace('ggpaintr')), never a lexical reference, because under devtools::load_all() an exported function's closure rebinds to package:ggpaintr and a same-named empty shadow would diverge from the populated namespace copy — leaving every numeric-fold helper with an empty registry.
findings.detail: R/paintr-default-args.R:67-69: ptr_constant_fold_env_get() is exactly get('ptr_constant_fold_env', envir=asNamespace('ggpaintr')) so there is only ever one env regardless of load path. The hazard is documented in the comment 56-64. Every registry site routes through the accessor: register (107), clear (114), keywords (132), builtins seeder (138), walker (156), fold (194). Same load_all registry-split hazard seen elsewhere in the project (probe/devtools-vs-pkgload memory).
findings.verification: accessor get('ptr_constant_fold_env', asNamespace('ggpaintr')) at 67-69; hazard comment 56-64; all six access sites route through it; no stray lexical refs.

findings.claim: ptr_signal_partial(message) signals a 'ptr_partial_input'-classed condition (rlang::abort with that class) caught ONLY on the LIVE keystroke-driven reactive boundaries (consumer-picker prefetch returns NULL; renderUI entry_reactive returns shiny::req(FALSE, cancelOutput=TRUE)) and DELIBERATELY NOT on the Update/Draw-gated plot path — so mid-typing a free-text widget keeps pickers from strobing blank while a still-partial value at draw time surfaces as a normal inline error. Hooks should use it for mid-typing artifacts and ordinary rlang::abort() for real (security/arg-shape) errors.
findings.detail: ptr_signal_partial = rlang::abort(message, class='ptr_partial_input', ...) (R/paintr-registry.R:961-963); roxygen 927-960 states the asymmetric-catch contract verbatim. Catch sites: prefetch tryCatch around ptr_resolve_upstream → ptr_partial_input=function(e) NULL (server.R:2102-2113, skips consumer like is.null(df)); renderUI entry_reactive tryCatch → ptr_partial_input=function(e) shiny::req(FALSE, cancelOutput=TRUE) (server.R:2859-2872). The gated plot-build path installs no such handler, so the partial value re-hits during substitute (ptr_complete_expr_safe's tryCatch(error=) → ok=FALSE) and is reported inline. ppExpr resolve (builtins:139-172) signals partial on empty/non-character/unparseable/multi-expr input.
findings.verification: ptr_signal_partial = rlang::abort(message, class='ptr_partial_input', ...) (R/paintr-registry.R:980-982). Catch site 1 (prefetch around ptr_resolve_upstream): ptr_partial_input = function(e) NULL at R/paintr-server.R:2121, with the comment at 2108-2110 stating substitute will re-hit the condition and report it inline. Catch site 2 (renderUI entry_reactive): ptr_partial_input = function(e) shiny::req(FALSE, cancelOutput=TRUE) at R/paintr-server.R:2893-2895. The Update/Draw-gated plot-build path installs no such handler, so a still-partial value surfaces as an inline error — confirmed by the prefetch comment and absence of a third handler. ppExpr resolver still signals partial on empty/non-character/unparseable/multi-expr (R/paintr-builtins.R). The safe_to_remove fix only added a param to the ptr_resolve_upstream call inside the tryCatch (server.R:2118, 2890); the asymmetric-catch contract is untouched. Refs drifted (2121, 2893) but stay within the cited symbol regions. Claim holds.

findings.claim: The ggpaintr LLM-assist surface is four exported functions in R/paintr-llm.R that are pure file readers over inst/llm/ — they emit no live introspection, just bundled markdown: ptr_llm_primer() (primer.md), ptr_llm_topics() (lists topics/*.md), ptr_llm_topic(name) (reads topics/<name>.md), ptr_llm_register(chat, tool_name='ggpaintr_docs') (wraps ptr_llm_topic as an ellmer tool). 12 topics ship today.
findings.detail: All four locate files via system.file('llm',...,package='ggpaintr') and abort with typed condition classes (ggpaintr_llm_primer_missing/topics_missing/topic_unknown/ellmer_missing/bad_chat) when an asset or dependency is absent. Intended use: feed primer as system_prompt, register the docs tool so the model pulls one focused runnable example on demand. The 12 topics: custom_placeholder, formula_syntax, level1_ptr_app, level1_ptr_options, level2_custom_ids, level2_module, level2_shared, level2_ui_text, level3_custom_render, level3_gg_extra, level3_layout, overview.
findings.verification: Read R/paintr-llm.R (~20-194): ptr_llm_primer() reads system.file('llm','primer.md',...); ptr_llm_topics() lists topics/*.md; ptr_llm_topic(name) reads topics/<name>.md; ptr_llm_register(chat, tool_name='ggpaintr_docs') wraps ptr_llm_topic as an ellmer tool. Each aborts with typed classes (ggpaintr_llm_*_missing/unknown/ellmer_missing/bad_chat). Confirmed inst/llm/topics holds 12 .md topic files (the 12 named topics: custom_placeholder, formula_syntax, level1_ptr_app, level1_ptr_options, level2_custom_ids, level2_module, level2_shared, level2_ui_text, level3_custom_render, level3_gg_extra, level3_layout, overview). Claim holds.

findings.claim: The LLM-assist content (primer.md + 11 of the 12 topic files) tells the model to call the UNEXPORTED names ptr_module_ui / ptr_module_server, while NAMESPACE exports only ptr_ui / ptr_server for the L2 default-layout pair — content drift baked into shipped markdown. An LLM following the primer/topics will emit calls that fail. The one exception: the ptr_llm_register tool-enum description (R/paintr-llm.R:174) correctly names 'ptr_ui / ptr_server'.
findings.detail: NAMESPACE exports ptr_ui (120), ptr_server (115), ptr_shared/_panel/_server (116-118); NO ptr_module_* export (the only ptr_module_ mention in R/ is a stale comment in R/paintr-build-ui.R:4). grep ptr_module across inst/llm returns 12 files = primer.md + 11 topics (level1_ptr_options.md is the only topic without it). primer.md uses ptr_module_ui/ptr_module_server throughout and explicitly frames them as 'the deliberate sealed-bundle exception' (line 24). The content is hand-maintained markdown with no test pinning function names against NAMESPACE.
findings.verification: Confirmed NAMESPACE exports ptr_ui and ptr_server (and ptr_shared/_panel/_server) but NO ptr_module_* export. inst/llm content references the unexported ptr_module_ui/ptr_module_server in primer.md plus 11 topic files (grep returned 12 files under inst/llm, only level1_ptr_options.md without it). The one correct spot is the ptr_llm_register tool-enum description in R/paintr-llm.R which names 'ptr_ui / ptr_server'. The stale R/paintr-build-ui.R top-of-file comment mentioning ptr_module is also present. Content drift is real today; claim holds.

findings.claim: ptr_llm_register() snapshots the valid topic names once (via ptr_llm_topics()) into an ellmer type_enum at registration time (so the LLM cannot request a nonexistent topic, but the enum goes stale on a mid-session ggpaintr upgrade), and its chat guard is LENIENT — it accepts anything inheriting 'Chat' OR 'R6' OR being an environment, deferring the real contract (a $register_tool method) to the later chat$register_tool() call.
findings.detail: topic_names<-ptr_llm_topics() captured (R/paintr-llm.R:151) and passed to ellmer::type_enum(topic_names,...) constraining the tool's name arg (164-188); roxygen 103-106 flags that upgrading+adding topics in the same session needs a fresh ptr_llm_register on a fresh chat. ptr_llm_topic() independently re-validates name and aborts ggpaintr_llm_topic_unknown (79-89). The chat guard (143-149) aborts ggpaintr_llm_bad_chat only when chat is none of {inherits Chat, inherits R6, is.environment} — so any R6 object/environment passes; real enforcement is implicit at line 192 chat$register_tool(tool_def). ellmer availability checked first via requireNamespace (128); tool_name asserted single non-empty string (138-142).
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-llm.R::ptr_llm_register. topic_names <- ptr_llm_topics() captured once (line 151) and passed to ellmer::type_enum(topic_names,...) constraining the tool's name arg (164). ptr_llm_topic (72-) independently re-validates name and aborts ggpaintr_llm_topic_unknown (87). The chat guard (143-147) aborts ggpaintr_llm_bad_chat only when chat is none of {inherits 'Chat', inherits 'R6', is.environment} -- lenient; real enforcement is implicit at chat$register_tool(tool_def) (line 192). requireNamespace('ellmer') checked first (128). Roxygen (103-106) flags the stale-enum-on-upgrade caveat. Claim holds essentially verbatim; line numbers barely drifted.

findings.claim: primer.md is not a generic doc dump -- it encodes the project's load-bearing API-routing rules: the ptr_<x>=L2-self-contained vs ptr_ui_<x>=L3-bare prefix convention (the bare-named ptr_ui / ptr_server are the L2 default-layout pair), the 'pick the LOWEST level that covers the need' decision rule, the no-`placeholders=`-argument / process-global-registry invariant with its three ptr_define_placeholder_* constructors and five protected built-ins, the L3 custom-render contract (read state$runtime() in reactive blocks; reserve ptr_extract_* for non-reactive contexts), and ptr_gg_extra replace-not-append semantics -- a second hand-synced copy of the API contract mirrored into the LLM channel.
findings.detail: prefix rule at primer.md:19-24 (ptr_module_* called out as the deliberate sealed-bundle exception, 24); 'pick the LOWEST' header at 26 + a 4-bullet routing list mapping user intent to L1/L2/L3/custom-placeholder at 100-105; NO placeholders= argument at 35/91 with the three ptr_define_placeholder_* constructors (93-95) and 5 unclearable built-ins (96,132); L3 custom-render reactivity contract at 85/127; gg_extra replace-not-append at 86/128. overview.md:1-15 restates the prefix + 3-level model. Mirrors CLAUDE.md/CONTEXT.md invariants into the LLM channel.
findings.verification: Re-read inst/llm/primer.md + inst/llm/topics/overview.md. Core claim holds: prefix rule (ptr_<x>=L2 self-contained / ptr_ui_<x>=L3 bare) at primer.md:23-26; 'pick the LOWEST that covers the need' header at 30; no `placeholders =` argument / process-global registry at 37 and 108-109; three ptr_define_placeholder_* constructors at 111-113 (now carrying the ADR-0027 arg names parse_positional_arg/parse_named_args/validate_session_input/embellish_eval); five protected built-ins at 114 ('The five built-ins are protected'); L3 custom-render contract (read state$runtime() in reactive blocks, reserve ptr_extract_* for non-reactive) at 94 and 147; ptr_gg_extra replace-not-append at 104 and 148. overview.md:1-15 restates the prefix + 3-level model. ONE drift in the finding's DETAIL: the 'ptr_module_* called out as the deliberate sealed-bundle exception at line 24' no longer exists -- the L2 default-layout pair is now the bare-named ptr_ui / ptr_server (primer.md:25), there is no ptr_module_* exception.
