findings.claim: ppExpr/expr safety is enforced at FOUR independent points in the pipeline, not once at parse time, because resolve hooks can synthesize new code and post-substitution structure differs from the original formula: (1) translate-time over the whole typed tree (ptr_validate_tree_safety), (2) after a resolve hook returns language/expression (validate_resolve_expr_return), (3) in ptr_resolve_upstream before eval'ing a data-producing expr, (4) in ptr_eval re-validating each per-layer eval expression immediately before eval().
findings.detail: The walker is the gate in front of every eval() call, re-applied because substitution/resolution can reshape the AST. (1) ptr_translate calls ptr_validate_tree_safety(root, expr_check) at R/paintr-translate.R:89, defined in R/paintr-safety.R:8-15; (2) re-screened in validate_resolve_expr_return (R/paintr-substitute.R:297-312, comment 'resolve hooks can synthesize new calls ... screen them again'); (3) in ptr_resolve_upstream (R/paintr-resolve.R:42-43, NOT a function named ptr_resolve_data — no such function); (4) in ptr_eval re-validating each expr (R/paintr-eval.R:18-22) before eval(e, envir=eval_env) (line 23).
findings.verification: All four independent screening points confirmed, and critically the resolve.R point still PRECEDES eval after the safe_to_remove threading fix. (1) translate-time: ptr_translate calls ptr_validate_tree_safety(root, expr_check) at R/paintr-translate.R:89; defined R/paintr-safety.R:8. (2) post-resolve-hook: validate_resolve_expr_return (R/paintr-substitute.R:287) calls validate_expr_safety (305,309) with comment 'hooks can synthesize new calls ... the denylist must screen them again' (299-300). (3) ptr_resolve_upstream now takes safe_to_remove (R/paintr-resolve.R:26) and is_standalone (27) and prunes at 41-42, but validate_expr_safety (55) STILL runs BEFORE eval(expr, envir=eval_env) (59) — the safe_to_remove fix did NOT move safety after eval. (4) ptr_eval re-validates each expr via validate_expr_safety (R/paintr-eval.R:21) before eval(e, envir=eval_env) (23). No function named ptr_resolve_data exists (correct). Claim holds; resolve.R line refs drifted from 42-43 to 55-59.

findings.claim: The recursive AST walker (walk_expr, inside validate_expr_safety) is the PRIMARY safety mechanism: it descends into pairlists, character vectors/string literals, bare symbols, namespaced calls, and compound/computed call heads, so dangerous names are caught no matter where in the tree they appear — the denylist is just the membership set it consults.
findings.detail: walk_expr is a closure inside validate_expr_safety (R/paintr-utils.R:624-727; defined 654, invoked 725). It dispatches on node kind: is.pairlist→recurse each element (walks closure formals + body); is.character→check_name on every string element (blocks a denied name as string literal / character-vector element, a get()/match.fun() bypass vector); is.symbol→check_name (blocks a bare denied name); is.call→if head x[[1]] is itself a call recurse into it FIRST (693-695), then extract_fn_names on the head + denylist/allowlist check (697-720), then walk every remaining argument (722). extract_fn_names (598-612) returns both bare and qualified name for ::/::: heads.
findings.verification: Walker structure confirmed: pairlist/character/symbol/call branches; compound-head recursion 693-695; argument walk 722; extract_fn_names returns c(bare,qualified) for ::/::: (603-608).

findings.claim: Within a call node the walker recurses a compound/computed head BEFORE checking the head name, then checks the head against the deny/allow list, then walks the arguments — so neither `(system)('x')` nor a denied call wrapping arguments can slip a forbidden name past the check; the one intentional hole is the early-return skip of registered placeholder keywords (from ptr_registry_keywords()).
findings.detail: Order in the is.call branch (R/paintr-utils.R:677-723): (a) early-return if head symbol is a registered placeholder keyword (681-684) — formula DSL, not user code; (b) if head x[[1]] is itself a call, recurse into it first (693-695), catching a denied symbol inside a computed head like (system); (c) extract_fn_names + denylist/allowlist check on the head (697-720); (d) walk each remaining argument (722). NOTE: the skip-keyword set is supplied via ptr_registry_keywords() (passed as placeholder_names); the in-code comment at 679 names parse_placeholder_token as the parse-time validator, but that token exists ONLY in the comment — no function by that name exists in R/.
findings.verification: Read the is.call branch of check_call in R/paintr-utils.R (~668-723): (a) early-return when the head symbol is a registered placeholder keyword (placeholder_names, sourced from ptr_registry_keywords() per R/paintr-registry.R ~121); (b) if head x[[1]] is itself a call, recurse into it FIRST (catching a denied symbol in a computed head like (system)); (c) extract head fn names and apply deny/allow check; (d) walk remaining arguments. Order = recurse-compound-head -> check-head -> walk-args, with the sole intentional hole being the placeholder-keyword skip. Claim holds. Caveat noted in finding detail (parse_placeholder_token exists only in a comment) remains accurate.

findings.claim: expr_check accepts TRUE (default denylist), FALSE (all validation off), or a list with deny_list/allow_list; supplying allow_list flips the walker to DEFAULT-DENY (only listed names pass) AND the curated unsafe_expr_denylist (151 entries, lockBinding'd) still hard-blocks always-dangerous bare names even in allowlist mode.
findings.detail: resolve_expr_check (R/paintr-utils.R:555-587): FALSE→mode 'off'; TRUE→mode 'denylist' with the built-in unsafe_expr_denylist; a list with neither allow_list nor deny_list (incl. empty list())→falls back to default denylist (a documented footgun, @note — pass TRUE explicitly); allow_list present→mode 'allowlist' with fns = allow_list minus any deny_list; deny_list only→mode 'denylist' with the user's list REPLACING the built-in. In allowlist mode deny_hit() still checks bare symbols against unsafe_expr_denylist (635-641), and a call head whose names aren't in the allowlist aborts (711-720). unsafe_expr_denylist is lockBinding'd (544), immutable at runtime, 151 entries.
findings.verification: All resolve_expr_check branches; deny_hit checks denylist in allowlist mode 638-640; allowlist call-head abort 711-720; lockBinding 544; counted denylist = 151 entries.

findings.claim: expr_check=FALSE is a total bypass: both safety entry points return invisible(TRUE) up front (mode=='off'), so user free-form expressions reach eval() with NO denylist and NO walker — meaning expr_check=FALSE makes ppExpr equivalent to letting the user run arbitrary R in the app's process; only use for fully trusted input.
findings.detail: ptr_validate_tree_safety returns before walking (R/paintr-safety.R:10-12) and validate_expr_safety returns invisible(TRUE) before walk_expr (R/paintr-utils.R:628-630). ptr_eval then runs eval(e, envir=eval_env) (R/paintr-eval.R:23) with eval_env carrying upload-bound names. With default TRUE the walker + 151-entry denylist block system escape, file I/O, meta-eval (eval/parse/get/do.call/match.fun all in the denylist), env/global-state mutation, and introspection across all node positions; allow_list opens exactly the named functions; FALSE removes the boundary entirely. Documented @param on ptr_app (app.R:17) and ptr_server (server.R:31).
findings.verification: Read both safety entry points: ptr_validate_tree_safety (R/paintr-safety.R) returns invisible(TRUE) up front when mode=='off' (expr_check=FALSE); validate_expr_safety (R/paintr-utils.R ~624-630) returns invisible(TRUE) before calling walk_expr when check is off. ptr_eval (R/paintr-eval.R) then runs eval(e, envir=eval_env) with eval_env carrying upload-bound names. With default TRUE the walker (walk_expr -> check_call, R/paintr-utils.R 632-723) + deny/allow check gates every call node. expr_check=FALSE is therefore a total bypass = arbitrary R in-process. @param documented on ptr_app and ptr_server. Claim holds.

findings.claim: Two layered walkers exist: the S3 walk_ptr_safety visitor walks the typed ptr_* tree and delegates each node's `expr` (or ptr_user_expr's `inner`) field to the language-level validate_expr_safety, while placeholder-value/source/consumer/missing nodes are deliberately no-ops in the tree visitor (their resolved output is re-screened later at resolve time).
findings.detail: walk_ptr_safety (R/paintr-safety.R:17-90) is a UseMethod visitor: ptr_root recurses layers (22-27); ptr_layer/ptr_pipeline/ptr_call/ptr_literal each call validate_expr_safety(node$expr,...) (30-55); ptr_user_expr validates node$inner — the raw user expression from ppExpr (57-62); ptr_ph_value/ptr_ph_data_consumer/ptr_ph_data_source/ptr_missing return invisible(TRUE) with no check (64-82); an unknown class aborts via the default method (84-90). So the tree visitor handles structure/dispatch and the language walker (validate_expr_safety in R/paintr-utils.R) handles deny/allow enforcement on each expr.
findings.verification: Visitor confirmed verbatim: root recurses, layer/pipeline/call/literal delegate node$expr, user_expr delegates node$inner, ph_*/missing no-op, default aborts. Language walker lives in paintr-utils.R.

findings.claim: ptr_translate's annotate=TRUE block runs a fixed 4-pass annotation order — ptr_classify_calls (lift nested chains to ptr_pipeline) → ptr_assign_ids → ptr_classify_data → ptr_shared_bind — and the order is load-bearing, not cosmetic (with ptr_assert_no_surviving_structural_wrappers interposed between classify_calls and assign_ids).
findings.detail: R/paintr-translate.R:90-118. Four passes at 101/109/113/114; assertion at 108; comment 91-100 documents both constraints: (1) assign_ids AFTER classify_calls so structural-position-derived ids are stamped on the canonical post-lift shape (else nested-call and %>% users get DIFFERENT ids for the same widget); (2) classify_data AFTER assign_ids because R lists are copy-on-modify — the `upstream` reference classify_data pins would otherwise point at pre-id nodes (frozen at id=NA). Before annotation, translate desugars all pipes to nested form (desugar_pipes_to_nested, 70) so per-layer translation never sees a pipe head; lifting is solely ptr_classify_calls's job.
findings.verification: Read ptr_translate (R/paintr-translate.R ~21-127) annotate block: desugar_pipes_to_nested first (so per-layer translation never sees a pipe head), then the 4-pass order ptr_classify_calls -> ptr_assign_ids -> ptr_classify_data -> ptr_shared_bind, with ptr_assert_no_surviving_structural_wrappers interposed between classify_calls and assign_ids. The in-source comment documents both ordering constraints (assign_ids after classify_calls for canonical-shape ids; classify_data after assign_ids due to copy-on-modify of the upstream reference). All confirmed by symbol; claim holds.

findings.claim: The three pipe surface forms (|>, %>%, nested-call) all produce a structurally identical typed pipeline; the only difference is a render-time `op` annotation, never tree shape (ADR-0012 §1).
findings.detail: op_hint is derived once at translate time by ptr_first_pipe_op (R/paintr-translate.R:12-19): pick whichever of %>% / |> appears first in the source, default |> when neither present. Threaded as a pure render-time slot (op= on ptr_pipeline; source_pipe_op on ptr_layer) and never affects tree structure. desugar_pipes_to_nested (230-254) erases pipe operators by inserting LHS as RHS's first positional arg, preserving arg names. Native |> is preprocessed to the %ptrPipeNative% sentinel BEFORE parsing (preprocess_native_pipe, 522-543) using getParseData PIPE token positions so |> inside strings/comments is never touched. Render side: render_walk.ptr_layer keys the asymmetric chain branch off node$source_pipe_op=='%>%' (R/paintr-render.R:48-76).
findings.verification: Read ptr_first_pipe_op (R/paintr-translate.R ~12-19): picks whichever of %>% / |> appears first, default |>. desugar_pipes_to_nested (~230-254) erases pipe operators by inserting LHS as RHS first positional arg, preserving arg names -> identical tree shape across surface forms. preprocess_native_pipe (~522-543) rewrites native |> to a %ptrPipeNative% sentinel before parsing using getParseData PIPE token positions (so |> in strings/comments untouched). Render side render_walk.ptr_layer (R/paintr-render.R ~48-76) keys the asymmetric branch off node$source_pipe_op=='%>%'. op is a pure render-time slot; claim (ADR-0012 sec1) holds.

findings.claim: The nested-chain-to-pipeline lift (try_lift_to_pipeline) fires only when three independent gates all pass: GATE 0 at least one stage above the source; GATE 1 a round-trip-identical split/rebuild; GATE 2 a source slot that is a bare SYMBOL or a SOURCE-role (data-producing) placeholder call -- a value/consumer placeholder is rejected. Two helpers split the duty: is_placeholder_call() is the all-roles atomicity guard for the descent loop (treats any non-structural registered placeholder as an atomic leaf, role=='structural' returns FALSE so the source-split still fires on structural wrappers), while is_data_source_placeholder_call() governs source ACCEPTANCE (true only for a SOURCE-role placeholder). Any gate failure leaves the data-arg unchanged.
findings.detail: R/paintr-translate.R:379-396. GATE 0 (non-empty): length(parts$stages)<1→'no-stages'; GATE 1 (round-trip): !identical(rebuilt,canonical)→'round-trip-mismatch'; GATE 2 (grounding): a call source that is NOT a placeholder call→'opaque-call-source'; a non-symbol non-placeholder→'non-data-source'. is_placeholder_call (342-357) treats ANY registered value/consumer/source placeholder as an atomic leaf, so resugar_pipeline_stages's post-loop source-split skips it (286-287); structural keywords have role=='structural' and return FALSE, so the source-split still fires on them.
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-translate.R::try_lift_to_pipeline (412-429). Three gates fire in order: GATE 0 length(parts$stages)<1L -> 'no-stages' (415); GATE 1 !identical(rebuilt,canonical) -> 'round-trip-mismatch' (419); GATE 2 source grounding: is.call(parts$source) && !is_data_source_placeholder_call(parts$source) -> 'opaque-call-source' (422), and !is.symbol(parts$source) && !is_data_source_placeholder_call(parts$source) -> 'non-data-source' (425). is_placeholder_call (343-358) still treats any non-structural registered placeholder as atomic for the descent loop and returns FALSE on role=='structural' (356), so resugar_pipeline_stages's source-split still fires on structural wrappers (268,288). DRIFT (post-finding narrowing, fix-expr era): GATE 2's source ACCEPTANCE no longer uses is_placeholder_call -- a NEW helper is_data_source_placeholder_call (369-) restricts an accepted call-source to SOURCE-role placeholders only (e.g. ppUpload), so a value/consumer placeholder (ppExpr/ppVar) is rejected as a source. The finding's GATE-2 prose '(or a non-structural placeholder call)' is now too broad.

findings.claim: classify_data is an S3 visitor (classify_walk) that threads a ctx_data AST-pointer through the typed tree and stamps it as `upstream` on every ptr_ph_data_consumer; data sources clear their upstream, and the ggplot layer's data_arg becomes the default context for all sibling layers.
findings.detail: R/paintr-classify.R:6-103. classify_walk.ptr_root (16-37) finds the first 'ggplot' layer, walks it, and if it has a data_arg promotes that to default_data for every other layer. classify_walk.ptr_layer (40-55) overrides children's context with its own data_arg when present. classify_walk.ptr_pipeline (58-76): stage 1 gets incoming ctx; stage 2 gets node$stages[[1]] directly; stage 3+ gets a ptr_pipeline of stages[1..i-1]. Only consumers record upstream (90); sources null it (96-97); value placeholders/literals/unrecognised pass unchanged via classify_walk.default. The upstream pointer is an AST subtree, not materialized data (header notes materialization is P12's job).
findings.verification: Read 1-103: root ggplot-first + default_data promotion, layer override, pipeline stage-ctx threading with stage-2 passthrough, consumer upstream stamp 90, source null 96-97, default unchanged.

findings.claim: substitute_walk resolves every placeholder from the runtime input_snapshot (or the shared binding) alone and NEVER falls back to node$default — node$default is a boot-time UI seed consumed elsewhere, so once Shiny boots the snapshot is the single source of truth.
findings.detail: R/paintr-substitute.R:1-14,90-263. Each placeholder→ptr_literal (resolved value), ptr_user_expr (only ppExpr, verbatim provenance, 125-127), or ptr_missing (input absent/empty/NA). read_placeholder_value (253-263) reads ctx$shared[[node$shared]] (value or reactive function it calls) when a shared key is set, else ctx$snapshot[[node$id]] — never node$default. Missing-ness keyword-specific (is_missing_value_input, 265-285): ppNum treats all-NA/blank as missing; ppText/ppExpr treat '' as missing so labs(title=ppText()) collapses. resolve_expr returns type-validated (287-313) and returned language re-run through the P5 safety walker (validate_expr_safety, utils.R:624) because resolve hooks can synthesize new calls.
findings.verification: Header 8-14 states no node$default fallback; read_placeholder_value shared-then-snapshot only; is_missing_value_input keyword-specific; ppExpr→ptr_user_expr; validate_resolve_expr_return re-runs validate_expr_safety (defined utils.R:624).

findings.claim: ptr_render has two modes: eval-mode (default, placeholders already substituted) renders pipelines verbatim, while preserve-mode (preserve_placeholders=TRUE) emits surviving placeholders as round-trippable ppX(pick) and applies an asymmetric prefix-collapse so lifted placeholder-free pipelines stay stable.
findings.detail: R/paintr-render.R:27-29,99-167. Eval-mode (render_pipeline_body, 102-113) renders every stage as a |> / %>% chain verbatim. Preserve-mode (115-167) keys off the first stage index k containing a placeholder: k==NA → %>% preserves on sight as a chain but |> collapses the whole pipeline to a nested-call atom; k<=2 → render full chain as-is; else stages[1..k-1] collapse to a nested-call source atom and stages[k..N] render as a chain from that prefix. Preserve-mode placeholder text is keyword-first (format_placeholder_value, 555-576: ppVar/ppUpload bareword, ppText quoted, ppNum numeric, ppExpr parsed-expr) with bare ppX (no parens) when neither current_pick nor shared is set, round-tripping back through ptr_translate. The %>%-preserve / |>-collapse asymmetry is preserve-mode ONLY.
findings.verification: Two-mode dispatch; eval-mode verbatim; preserve-mode three-branch k-logic (k==NA %>%-vs-|>, k<=2 full, mixed prefix-collapse via rebuild_nested_from_stages_typed); keyword-first format_placeholder_value + bare-ppX round-trip.

findings.claim: A placeholder's raw Shiny id is the 4-part string `<layer>_<underscore-joined-index-path>_<keyword>_<shared-or-NA>` (path '0' for a direct layer arg), stamped once in assign_id_to_placeholder during ptr_assign_ids' pre-order pass; the parent prefix is the enclosing function name, so a ppText in labs() is labs_1_ppText_NA and a ppVar in aes() under ggplot is ggplot_1_1_ppVar_NA.
findings.detail: R/paintr-ids.R:75-107: shared_part = node$shared or 'NA'; path_str = paste(path,collapse='_') or '0'; node$id = paste0(layer_name,'_',path_str,'_',node$keyword,'_',shared_part). STABILITY (input-spec.R:220-224 @section): adding a layer at the end keeps existing ids stable, but reordering args inside aes()/a pipeline shifts the positional path and changes the id; renaming the keyword or shared= annotation also changes it. Data-source nodes additionally get node$auto_name=paste0('df_',ptr_hash(node$id)) (ptr_hash = first 6 hex of rlang::hash, ids.R:25) for non-shared sources; a `<id>_shortcut` companion (node$shortcut_id) when the registry entry sets shortcut=TRUE.
findings.verification: Read assign_id_to_placeholder region of R/paintr-ids.R (~75-107): shared_part = node$shared or 'NA'; path_str = paste(path, collapse='_') or '0'; node$id = paste0(layer_name,'_',path_str,'_',node$keyword,'_',shared_part) -> 4-part <layer>_<index-path>_<keyword>_<shared|NA>. Data-source nodes additionally get node$auto_name = paste0('df_', ptr_hash(node$id)); ptr_hash (~25) = first 6 hex of rlang::hash; shortcut companion via node$shortcut_id when registry entry shortcut=TRUE. Stability @section in R/paintr-input-spec.R (~218-224) matches (append stable; reorder/rename/shared-change shifts id). Claim holds.

findings.claim: ptr_id_table(formula, id=NULL) is the L3 introspection enumerator returning one data.frame row per Shiny id ever rendered for a formula, where include_in_ui=FALSE marks exactly the two ids the user must NOT place by hand (the inner placeholder widget and ppUpload's file-name companion) because the server populates them inside the `<id>_ui` sleeve; the optional id= prefixes only scope=='instance' rows via shiny::NS(id).
findings.detail: R/paintr-input-spec.R:150-261. Rows cover static infra (ptr_update_plot/ptr_plot/ptr_error/ptr_code/ptr_code_mode), per-layer controls (layer_checkbox/content/subtab), placeholder sleeve+inner widgets (emit_placeholder_rows 335-367), source companions, and pipeline stage toggles. include_in_ui=FALSE set on exactly two row kinds: the inner placeholder widget row (raw_id) and the source companion row (ph$shortcut_id) — both server-rendered inside the sleeve via renderUI; the sleeve row (placeholder_output_id(raw_id)) carries include_in_ui=TRUE. Roxygen 207-210 names the two FALSE cases. This is the L3 hand-built-UI counterpart to the narrower server-only ptr_runtime_input_spec.
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-input-spec.R. ptr_id_table (def 239) walks the typed AST emitting one row per Shiny id; the optional id= prefixes scope=='instance' rows (roxygen 197). include_in_ui=FALSE is set on exactly two row kinds inside emit_placeholder_rows (335-): the inner placeholder widget row (id=raw_id, 354-355) and the source companion row (id=ph$shortcut_id, 361-362), both server-rendered inside the sleeve; the sleeve row (placeholder_output_id(raw_id)) carries include_in_ui=TRUE (349). All static-infra and per-layer control rows are include_in_ui=TRUE. Roxygen (206-210) names the two FALSE cases. ptr_runtime_input_spec (23-) is the narrower server-only counterpart (comment 157). Claim holds; line numbers drifted slightly.

findings.claim: ptr_init_state() is a state CONTAINER constructor (allocates the reactiveVals / typed-tree / eval-env) that deliberately does NOT attach pipeline/runtime observers — it is the entry point for driving the typed tree programmatically or under shiny::testServer(); the public ptr_server (R/paintr-app.R:1092) and its unexported plumbing ptr_server_internal (R/paintr-server.R:749-835, do.call(ptr_init_state,...) at 835) wire the observers on top of it.
findings.detail: R/paintr-server.R:84-122,203-316. ptr_init_state returns structure(list(...), class=c('ptr_state','list')) (316). Roxygen 11-25 states it is a state container that allocates reactives but does NOT attach pipeline/runtime observers, and to reach for it to drive the typed tree programmatically/under testServer(). CORRECTION: the convenience wrapper that calls ptr_init_state and attaches observers is ptr_server_internal (server.R:749-835) — the PUBLIC ptr_server is NOT at server.R:749-835; it is in R/paintr-app.R:1092. Defaults: envir=parent.frame(), expr_check=TRUE (FALSE disables, or a list customises deny_list/allow_list), producer_debounce_ms=NULL = auto mode (initial 0ms, flips to 300ms after 3 consecutive resolutions >150ms, back after 5 <80ms). shared=/draw_trigger=/shared_resolutions=/shared_stage_enabled=/plots= are multi-instance/grid hooks an outer wrapper supplies.
findings.verification: Serena ptr_init_state body (R/paintr-server.R:91-386) + roxygen (lines 11-25): documented verbatim as a 'state container, not a from-scratch reactive-app builder: it allocates the reactives but does not attach the pipeline / runtime observers ... Reach for ptr_init_state() directly when you want to drive the typed tree programmatically or exercise ggpaintr under shiny::testServer()'. Defaults confirmed in body: envir=parent.frame(), expr_check=TRUE; producer_debounce_ms=NULL => initial_debounce_ms=0L, debounce_mode='auto' (roxygen: starts 0ms, flips to 300ms after 3 resolutions >150ms, back after 5 <80ms). shared=/draw_trigger=/shared_resolutions=/shared_stage_enabled=/plots= all present as multi-instance/grid hook args. The wrapper that calls ptr_init_state then wires observers is ptr_server_internal (R/paintr-server.R:808-879: state<-ptr_init_state(...) then ptr_setup_pipelines/producer_inputs/stage_enabled/runtime/...). The PUBLIC ptr_server is in R/paintr-app.R:1092-1146 (moduleServer wrapper calling ptr_server_internal), NOT at server.R:749-835. Every structural claim holds; only line numbers drifted.

findings.claim: ptr_extract_plot/error/code read shiny::isolate(state$runtime()), which ptr_init_state() seeds as reactiveVal(NULL), so on a bare (un-driven) state they return NULL/NULL/'' until the runtime observer — wired ONLY by ptr_server() via ptr_setup_runtime() — has actually fired on an Update/Draw click.
findings.detail: state$runtime is shiny::reactiveVal(NULL) (R/paintr-server.R:205). Accessors: ptr_extract_plot=isolate(state$runtime())$plot (3670), _error $error (3674), _code formats format_code_with_extras(state$runtime(), state$extras_exprs(), prologue) inside isolate (3678-3683). ptr_init_state's roxygen says it allocates reactives but does NOT attach the pipeline/runtime observers (those live in ptr_setup_* wired by ptr_server()). load_all probe: shiny::isolate({s<-ptr_init_state('ggplot(mtcars,aes(x=wt,y=mpg))+geom_point()')}) → plot NULL, error NULL, code '' (empty). The observer is created by ptr_setup_runtime (1836) and only writes state$runtime(res) after triggered() is true (1941). L3: get state from ptr_server(), then click Update/Draw at least once before plot/code/error populate.
findings.verification: state$runtime is shiny::reactiveVal(NULL) in ptr_init_state (R/paintr-server.R:91-386, runtime=shiny::reactiveVal(NULL) confirmed in the state structure list). ptr_extract_plot = shiny::isolate(state$runtime())$plot (3691); ptr_extract_error = $error (3695); ptr_extract_code (3699-3729) isolates rt<-state$runtime() then format_code_with_extras(rt, state$extras_exprs(), prologue=emit_upload_prologue(state$active_uploads())). The extract roxygen (3658-3690) + ptr_init_state roxygen confirm runtime stays NULL (=> plot NULL / error NULL / code '') until the runtime observer (wired only by ptr_server via ptr_setup_runtime) fires. ptr_setup_runtime (1843-1956): observe_runtime() returns early unless triggered(), else state$runtime(res) (1929). Claim holds; refs drifted to 3691-3729 / 1843-1956.

findings.claim: All three ptr_extract_* wrap their read in shiny::isolate(), so calling them inside a render*{} block makes that output fire ONCE on mount and never re-render; to update on each Update-plot click, read state$runtime() directly inside the reactive context (which establishes the dependency). Reserve ptr_extract_* for non-reactive contexts: download handlers, testServer assertions, one-shot reads.
findings.detail: ptr_extract_plot/error are shiny::isolate(state$runtime())$<field> (R/paintr-server.R:3670,3674); ptr_extract_code wraps format_code_with_extras(...) in shiny::isolate (3678-3683). The 'Reactive contexts' roxygen (3649-3660) spells out the contract verbatim: each wraps its read in isolate(); 'Do not call these inside a render*{} block ... the render block fires once on mount and never again. Inside a reactive context, read state$runtime() directly'. This is the core L3 custom-render pattern: a custom renderer reads state$runtime()$plot inside render*({...}), not ptr_extract_plot().
findings.verification: Read R/paintr-server.R:3658-3729. ptr_extract_plot/error wrap reads in shiny::isolate() (3691,3695); ptr_extract_code wraps both runtime() and extras_exprs()/active_uploads() in shiny::isolate (3699-3729). The 'Reactive contexts' roxygen section (3670-3681) states verbatim: each wraps its read in shiny::isolate(); 'Do not call these inside a render*{} block ... the render block fires once on mount and never again. Inside a reactive context, read state$runtime() directly ... Reserve ptr_extract_* for non-reactive contexts: download handlers, shiny::testServer() assertions, and one-shot reads'. Claim holds verbatim; refs drifted to 3658-3729.

findings.claim: The runtime value the L3 accessors read is a single detached list produced by ptr_exec_headless with fields ok/stage/error/plot/code_text/pruned (plus eval_env/condition, and a snapshot stamped by the observer), so a custom renderer pulls plot/error/code straight off state$runtime()$ok/$plot/$error without re-running the pipeline.
findings.detail: ptr_exec_headless (R/paintr-headless.R:22-50) runs ptr_complete_expr_safe → inject_resolved_data_list → ptr_assemble_plot_safe → (+extras Reduce) → ptr_validate_plot_render_safe and returns the detached list. ptr_complete_expr_safe builds list(ok, stage, code_text, pruned, eval_env, plot, error, condition) (R/paintr-safe.R:45-65); $plot set by ptr_assemble_plot_safe via ptr_eval (NULL on failure); $error a stage-prefixed string (NULL on success). The reactive observer computes res<-ptr_exec_headless(...) (server.R:1922-1934), stamps res$snapshot (1940), then state$runtime(res) (1941). ptr_extract_code does NOT read $code_text alone — it re-runs format_code_with_extras over the runtime + state$extras_exprs() + emit_upload_prologue(state$active_uploads()) prologue (3678-3683). The l3-plotly fixture reads state$runtime()$ok/$plot in renderPlotly (l3-plotly/app.R:19-24).
findings.verification: Serena ptr_exec_headless body (R/paintr-headless.R:22-50): res<-ptr_complete_expr_safe(tree,state,snapshot) -> early-return if !ok -> res$pruned<-inject_resolved_data_list(res$pruned, res$resolved_frames) -> ptr_assemble_plot_safe -> Reduce(`+`,extras,res$plot) when isTRUE(res$ok)&&length(extras)>0L -> ptr_validate_plot_render_safe; returns the detached list. The runtime observer (ptr_setup_runtime 1922-1929) computes res<-ptr_exec_headless(isolate(state$tree()),state,extras=state$extras(),snapshot), sets res$snapshot<-snapshot, then state$runtime(res). ptr_extract_code (3699-3729) re-runs format_code_with_extras over runtime+extras_exprs+emit_upload_prologue, NOT $code_text alone -- matches the finding. Result fields (ok/stage/error/plot/code_text/pruned + eval_env/condition) confirmed via ptr_complete_expr_safe(40-78) wrapping ptr_complete_expr and ptr_error_result. NOTE: the detail's literal 'list(...) at safe.R:45-65' drifted (now ptr_complete_expr_safe is a tryCatch wrapper at 40-78; ptr_exec_headless uses res$resolved_frames not a 'frames' arg) -- but the runtime-result-shape claim holds. l3-plotly fixture (app.R:21-23) reads state$runtime() then req(isTRUE(res$ok),res$plot) in renderPlotly -- corroborates.

findings.claim: R/paintr-headless.R (ptr_exec_headless, ptr_run_formula, ptr_headless_upstream_cols, ptr_default_snapshot) is INTERNAL ONLY — none is exported in NAMESPACE or has a man/ page; the only public no-Shiny-session reads are the isolate-wrapped ptr_extract_*, which still require a state whose runtime was driven inside a Shiny reactive. Consistent with ADR-0006: there is no supported batch/headless render.
findings.detail: File header (R/paintr-headless.R:1-8): 'Headless runtime — INTERNAL ONLY. ... Nothing here is exported or documented as public API'. Verified: grep of NAMESPACE finds none of the four names; ls man/ shows no headless/run_formula/exec_headless page. These helpers back the reactive observer (ptr_setup_runtime→ptr_exec_headless) and out-of-Shiny regression tests (ptr_run_formula(formula, inputs=list(), ...) 113-130, paired with ptr_default_snapshot 89-108), not a user batch path.
findings.verification: R/paintr-headless.R header still reads 'Headless runtime - INTERNAL ONLY ... Nothing here is exported or documented as public API' (lines 1-8). All four fns present: ptr_exec_headless (22), ptr_headless_upstream_cols (59, which gained a safe_to_remove=NULL param at 63 but stays internal), ptr_default_snapshot (91), ptr_run_formula (115). grep NAMESPACE for headless/run_formula/exec_headless/default_snapshot -> NONE exported. ls man/ -> NO man page for any of them. ADR-0006 no supported batch/headless render still holds; the only public no-session reads remain the isolate-wrapped ptr_extract_*. The safe_to_remove param addition on ptr_headless_upstream_cols did not change export status. Claim holds.

findings.claim: L3 has two mutually-exclusive composition modes on a given output slot: ptr_ui_plot() + ptr_server() lets ggpaintr OWN the plot output (ptr_server binds ptr_plot/ptr_error/ptr_code, return value ignored), whereas dropping ptr_ui_plot and supplying your own output id requires state<-ptr_server() and rendering from state$runtime() yourself.
findings.detail: l3-pieces/app.R:10-21 lays out ptr_ui_header/ptr_ui_controls/ptr_ui_plot/ptr_ui_error/ptr_ui_code and calls ptr_server(formula) with NO captured return (inline comment 'binds ptr_plot / ptr_error / ptr_code'). l3-plotly/app.R:10-24 omits ptr_ui_plot, substitutes plotly::plotlyOutput(shiny::NS('plot1')('custom_plot')), and captures state to feed its own renderPlotly. The two modes own the output slot differently.
findings.verification: Read l3-plotly/app.R (the 'own-render' mode): omits ptr_ui_plot, uses plotly::plotlyOutput(shiny::NS('plot1')('custom_plot')), captures state<-ptr_server(formula,'plot1') and renders output[[NS('plot1')('custom_plot')]]<-renderPlotly({res<-state$runtime(); req(isTRUE(res$ok),res$plot); ggplotly(res$plot)}). The 'ggpaintr owns the slot' mode is grounded in ptr_server_internal (R/paintr-server.R:808-879) calling ptr_register_plot/ptr_register_error/ptr_register_code, which bind ptr_plot/ptr_error/ptr_code when ptr_ui_plot is present and the return state is ignored. The two mutually-exclusive composition modes are confirmed. The l3-pieces fixture (the 'owns' example) was not separately re-read but the architecture is verified from ptr_server_internal's register calls + the l3-plotly capture mode.

findings.claim: Every L3 UI piece and the server share ONE namespace id passed as the second arg; custom outputs must be wrapped in shiny::NS(id)(...) to land in that namespace. ptr_ui_controls(formula,id)/ptr_ui_code(id)/ptr_server(formula,id) all key off the identical id string, and an un-namespaced ptr_ui_code() leaves the code panel empty.
findings.detail: l3-plotly uses 'plot1' uniformly: ptr_ui_controls(formula,'plot1') (app.R:12), ptr_ui_code('plot1') (15), plotly::plotlyOutput(shiny::NS('plot1')('custom_plot')) (13), ptr_server(formula,'plot1') (19), output[[shiny::NS('plot1')('custom_plot')]] (20). e2e confirms namespaced DOM ids: test-e2e:371-389 asserts plot1-ptr_update_plot, plot1-custom_plot, plot1-ggplot_1_1_ppVar_NA, plot1-ggplot_1_2_ppVar_NA, plot1-ptr_code — comment 378-379 'un-namespaced ptr_ui_code() left it empty'.
findings.verification: Read l3-plotly/app.R: id 'plot1' used uniformly -- ptr_ui_controls(formula,'plot1') (line 12), ptr_ui_code('plot1') (line 15), plotly::plotlyOutput(shiny::NS('plot1')('custom_plot')) (13), ptr_server(formula,'plot1') (19), output[[shiny::NS('plot1')('custom_plot')]] (20). ptr_server (R/paintr-app.R:1092-1146) sets id<-id%||%'ptr' and runs shiny::moduleServer(id,...), so server+UI pieces key off the one id; custom outputs must use shiny::NS(id)(...) to land in the namespace. Claim holds. The e2e DOM-id corroboration (test-e2e:367-391) was not re-read line-by-line this pass but the fixture + ptr_server moduleServer wiring ground the rule.

findings.claim: ptr_gg_extra(state, ...) is a programmatic layer-injection accessor with REPLACE-per-call semantics (each call overwrites prior extras, not append), evaluates all captured quosures up-front in state$eval_env so an eval error leaves existing extras untouched (atomic), and its presence (length(state$extras())>0) is one of the three OR'd runtime redraw triggers; folds extras into the plot via Reduce(`+`,extras,res$plot) ONLY when isTRUE(res$ok).
findings.detail: R/paintr-server.R:3711-3726: rlang::enquos(...) captured (3712), evaluated<-lapply(quos, rlang::eval_tidy, env=state$eval_env) runs (3717) BEFORE the writes state$extras_exprs(quos)/state$extras(evaluated) — a raising quosure aborts before either write (atomic). 'Replace-per-call' comment (3718). Returns invisible(state); empty ... short-circuits before any write. The runtime observer's triggered() reads update_clicked||draw_clicked||extras_present with NO || short-circuit (server.R:1848-1858, comment 'Read ALL three ... no || short-circuit'; extras_present=length(state$extras())>0L at 1856), and passes extras=state$extras() to ptr_exec_headless (1929). Plot fold: ptr_exec_headless does if(isTRUE(res$ok) && length(extras)>0L) res$plot<-Reduce(`+`,extras,res$plot) (headless.R:46-48) — extras are the EVALUATED ggplot objects. Code path is separate: format_code_with_extras deparses the stored extras_exprs quosures (quo_text), so plot uses evaluated objects while the code panel uses source quosures (two independent slots, kept consistent by ptr_gg_extra). An L3 renderer reading state$runtime()$plot gets extras already folded — do NOT re-add them. The l3-gg-extra e2e (test-e2e:393-419) proves scale_x_log10 injection changes the rendered (img_before != img_after).
findings.verification: Serena ptr_gg_extra body (R/paintr-server.R:3732-3747): quos<-rlang::enquos(...); empty => return(invisible(state)) (short-circuit before any write); evaluated<-lapply(quos, rlang::eval_tidy, env=state$eval_env) runs BEFORE state$extras_exprs(quos) and state$extras(evaluated) (comment: 'If any raises, we abort BEFORE mutating the reactiveVals ... atomic replace'); returns invisible(state). REPLACE-per-call confirmed (both reactiveVals overwritten). ptr_setup_runtime triggered() (1846-1855): reads update_clicked||draw_clicked||extras_present (extras_present=length(state$extras())>0L) with comment 'Read ALL three ... no || short-circuit'; passes extras=state$extras() to ptr_exec_headless (1925). Plot fold in ptr_exec_headless (headless.R:46-48): if(isTRUE(res$ok)&&length(extras)>0L) res$plot<-Reduce(`+`,extras,res$plot) -- evaluated ggplot objects. Code path separate: format_code_with_extras (3586-3601) deparses stored extras_exprs quosures via rlang::quo_text. All structural claims hold; line refs drifted (gg_extra 3711-3726 -> 3732-3747).

findings.claim: ptr_shared(formulas) builds one pure non-reactive ptr_shared_spec that is consumed by exactly three functions — ptr_shared_panel() (L2 self-contained UI), ptr_ui_shared_panel() (L3 bare UI), ptr_shared_server() — each taking only that object, so UI and server can never disagree on the partition. It is strictly MULTI-instance API: with a single ggpaintr instance all shared widgets auto-render inline and no coordinator is needed.
findings.detail: ptr_shared(formulas, id=NULL, ui_text=NULL, expr_check=TRUE, draw_all_label='Draw all'). `formulas` is a non-empty char vector OR list, one element per embedded ptr_ui() instance; each element is a formula string OR a quoted expr()/quote() (deparsed to source) — a *built* ggplot object is NOT accepted; native |> inside a quoted expr is desugared before capture so it doesn't survive into the code panel — use %>% or a string. Returns S3 ptr_shared_spec (class c('ptr_shared_spec','list')) with documented public fields panel_keys + local_keys_by_formula (full struct also carries id/formulas/trees/formula_count/formula_keys/keys_by_formula/firsts/consumer_reps/ui_text/expr_check/draw_all_label). ptr_shared() ERRORS (no shared= declared) when length(formula_keys)==0 — building the coordinator is a declaration of intent.
findings.verification: Re-verified at HEAD a1737f7. The sole intervening diff to the cited file(s) since this finding's baseline is b6a2f72 (the behavior-preserving partition_view seam, ADR 0026 #11), read in full: it only replaced three host_owned_keys derivations + one render_shared_section literal with equivalent partition_view accessor calls and added R/paintr-partition-view.R. It does not intersect this claim's code path; cited symbols and behavior confirmed unchanged. (Line numbers in R/paintr-app.R after ~1168 shifted +4 and in R/paintr-shared-ui.R after ~416 shifted +3; verify by symbol.)

findings.claim: The partition rule is purely a per-formula occurrence COUNT: a shared key referenced by >=2 formulas becomes ONE panel-owned widget with flat id `shared_<key>` rendered in the standalone always-visible ptr_shared_panel; a key referenced by exactly one formula renders inline in that formula's own shared section with the module-namespaced id `<module>-shared_<key>`. A key used twice INSIDE one formula still counts as one formula => formula-local.
findings.detail: shared_partition (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:28-51): keys_by_formula counts per-formula occurrence; panel_keys = all_keys[formula_counts>=2L] in discovery order; local_keys_by_formula[i] = keys occurring in exactly formula i. Dup-in-one-formula-counts-once via the per-formula unique(c(value_keys,consumer_keys)) set (26-27,29-34). Flat id = canonical_shared_id(key) = paste0('shared_',key) (R/paintr-shared.R:35); namespacing to <module>-shared_<key> for formula-local pickers comes from each ptr_server()'s NS(). e2e (l2-shared-partition test, test-e2e:264-280): formula-local plot_1-shared_ax1/plot_2-shared_ax2 populated while panel key sz is shared_sz and ABSENT under each module. USER guidance: reach for shared='key' to drive the same control across multiple panels; if only one formula uses the key it auto-renders inline anyway (the standalone panel will be empty of it).
findings.verification: Serena shared_partition body (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:28-75 -- file is paintr-shared-coordinator.R; the cited path was correct, the line range 28-51 drifted to 28-75): per_formula<-lapply(formula_keys, unique-as.character); formula_counts increments once per formula occurrence; panel_keys=all_keys where formula_counts[[k]]>=2L; local_keys_by_formula=per-formula keys with count<2L. Comment states verbatim 'A key referenced by >= 2 formulas is panel-owned ... a key in exactly one formula is formula-local'. per-formula unique() => a key used twice INSIDE one formula counts once => formula-local. Flat id confirmed: canonical_shared_id(key)=paste0('shared_',key) (R/paintr-ids.R:118, the cited ids.R:35 drifted to 118). Count-based partition claim holds exactly.

findings.claim: The shared_ui= argument on ptr_shared() (formerly a named list of function(id)->shiny.tag builders) is REMOVED; a shared key now always renders its representative placeholder's own build_ui, and per-widget customization comes from defining a custom placeholder (ptr_define_placeholder_*(build_ui=...)) and using it in the formula. Label-only divergence still has its own channel via node$shared_label.
findings.detail: ptr_shared() signature (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:245-249) has NO shared_ui param. @section 'Removed shared_ui' (206-218): it was redundant (each placeholder carries its own build_ui) and for var consumers actively dropped the formula default and froze the column list. The validation code is retained commented for provenance (280-305). shared_panel_body_tag no longer reads obj$shared_ui (354-372); the spec list intentionally omits the field (334-335). Sharing is declared purely by repeating shared='<key>' on a pp* placeholder across formulas — no separate registration; ptr_shared() aborts when no formula declares one. Keys live in one bucket: var/consumer keys in consumer_reps, value placeholders (num/text/expr/upload) in firsts (270-272). Fixtures grid-shared-partition (ppSize via _value) and app-grid-shared-added (ppMetric via _consumer) demonstrate the build_ui replacement with explicit shared_ui-removal headers.
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R. ptr_shared signature (245-249) is function(formulas, id=NULL, ui_text=NULL, expr_check=TRUE, draw_all_label='Draw all') -- NO shared_ui param. @section 'Removed `shared_ui`' (206-) explains the redundancy (each placeholder carries its own build_ui) and the var-consumer default-drop / frozen-column-list harm. The old validation block is retained commented for provenance (283-299). spec list intentionally omits the field (334-335 comment: 'shared_ui removed ... Field intentionally absent; shared_panel_body_tag() no longer reads obj$shared_ui'). Keys live in one bucket: var/consumer in consumer_reps, value placeholders in firsts (266-272). Both fixtures still exist and still contain shared_ui markers (grep matched grid-shared-partition/app.R and app-grid-shared-added/app.R). Claim holds verbatim.

findings.claim: ptr_shared_server(obj) must run at the top-level Shiny server (reads getDefaultReactiveDomain and aborts outside a reactive domain), and returns a ptr_shared_state the embedder threads into each ptr_server() via shared_state=; it builds reactives only for the cross-formula panel_keys (reading input[[ns(canonical_shared_id(key))]]) while formula-local keys are bound by each module's own ptr_server auto_bind_shared path — invariant: exactly one widget per shared key, correctly scoped.
findings.detail: Signature ptr_shared_server(obj, envir=parent.frame(), shared=list(), draw_trigger=NULL, spec=NULL) (R/paintr-shared-ui.R:260-264). `shared` overrides auto-derived reactives per key (must be reactives, unique non-empty names within formula_keys); `draw_trigger` overrides input$ptr_shared_draw_all; `spec` seeds host-owned panel widget defaults at boot (fully-qualified id->value). Returns ptr_shared_state with public fields shared/draw_trigger/shared_resolutions/shared_stage_enabled PLUS a fifth field panel_sources (ADR 0023; named list of reactive data.frames). getDefaultReactiveDomain abort-when-NULL (269-274). obj$id namespaces every emitted id (NS() separator); default NULL keeps flat ids for single-panel apps. shared_state threading validated/copied on ptr_server (server.R:767-821).
findings.verification: ptr_shared_server is at R/paintr-shared-ui.R:260 (the cited location is CORRECT; grep confirmed 'ptr_shared_server <- function(obj,' at shared-ui.R:260). It aborts when getDefaultReactiveDomain() is NULL: shared-ui.R:272 'ptr_shared_server() must be called inside a Shiny server function (no default reactive domain)' -- matches the claim's 'must run at the top-level Shiny server'. It requires a ptr_shared_spec from ptr_shared() (shared-ui.R:266). ptr_shared (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:244-341) builds part<-shared_partition(trees) and stores panel_keys/local_keys_by_formula, so the bundle's reactives cover only cross-formula panel_keys; formula-local keys are self-bound per module in ptr_server (R/paintr-app.R:1091-1233, the missing_keys/auto_bind_shared self-bind path) -- the 'exactly one widget per shared key, correctly scoped' invariant. The returned ptr_shared_state carries shared/draw_trigger/shared_resolutions/shared_stage_enabled + the 5th field panel_sources (ADR 0023), threaded into ptr_server via shared_state= (ptr_server seeds dots$shared/draw_trigger/shared_resolutions/shared_stage_enabled/panel_sources from the bundle, app.R:1097-1108; ptr_server_internal mirrors it at server.R:807-832). Every claim holds; the cited paintr-shared-ui.R:260 location is confirmed (it had drifted away from paintr-shared-coordinator.R but the finding's own ref was right).

findings.claim: ptr_shared_panel(obj, css=NULL) is the self-contained L2 renderer (wraps the panel in a .ptr-app themed scope plus the bundled ggpaintr asset dependency, drop-in ready), while ptr_ui_shared_panel(obj) is the L3 bare counterpart with identical inner wellPanel markup but NO shell and NO injected assets (the L3 user supplies their own shell/assets, e.g. via ptr_ui_page()). Both render exactly obj$panel_keys and return NULL when no panel keys exist; a Draw-all actionButton is added only when obj$formula_count>=2.
findings.detail: ptr_shared_panel (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:464-478): errors if not ptr_shared_spec, body=shared_panel_body_tag(obj, obj$panel_keys), returns NULL when body NULL, else tags$div class='ptr-app' wrapping ptr_assets(css) + body. ptr_ui_shared_panel (502-507): same guard, returns the bare body (no shell, no assets). The panel is a region fragment (sizes to content, bare .ptr-app with no --page canvas), meant to sit as its own row above the plots. Draw-all added when obj$formula_count>=2L (425-427).
findings.verification: ptr_shared_panel self-contained (.ptr-app + ptr_assets); ptr_ui_shared_panel bare body; both guard ptr_shared_spec + pass panel_keys + NULL on empty; Draw-all gated formula_count>=2.

findings.claim: Declaring shared='...' on a var/ppVar consumer makes its picker list the deepest pipeline level every occurrence agrees on (the bare data source if occurrences diverge; an error if even the source disagrees), and DELIBERATELY discards every upstream stage containing any placeholder — so a shared picker will NOT offer columns added by upstream mutate/select; to have a picker reflect its own evaluated pipeline you must DROP shared= and let the per-formula resolver feed it the live upstream.
findings.detail: resolve_shared_consumer (R/paintr-shared.R:276-324) truncates each occurrence's upstream at the first placeholder-bearing stage, then: sources differ→error; sources match + all truncated upstreams identical→use the upstream; sources match + upstreams differ→use the bare source. Two constraints force this: (a) the framework must never invoke a placeholder hook just to populate a dropdown (a custom resolve_expr may do I/O), (b) on divergence there is no canonical occurrence to source producer values from. Exception: a first-stage data-source placeholder (ppUpload) is kept as a valid runtime source, not truncated away (191-194,206-208). Separately, ptr_shared() fails fast at parse time (class ptr_panel_consumer_source_mismatch) when a panel-owned consumer key's upstream sources are formula-local or differ across formulas — fix = give the source the same shared= (ppUpload(shared='ds')) or drop shared= on the consumer.
findings.verification: Header 65-127 states the rule-of-thumb + tradeoff + constraints + do-not-share remedy; resolve_shared_consumer truncate/error/upstream/source branches; first-stage data-source kept; parse-time invariant shared_assert_panel_consumer_sources aborts ptr_panel_consumer_source_mismatch.

findings.claim: With a single ggpaintr instance, a shared= key reused multiple times in one formula auto-renders ONE inline widget (id shared_<key>) and drives every occurrence with no ptr_shared coordinator needed; it uses the standard ptr_update_plot draw button, NOT ptr_shared_draw_all.
findings.detail: Fixture single-instance-shared/app.R is a plain ptr_app() over aes(x=ppVar(shared='col'), y=ppVar-ppVar(shared='col'), color=Species) + geom_point() — one instance, key col used twice, no ptr_shared call. The ptr_shared roxygen states this verbatim: 'strictly multi-instance API: with a single ggpaintr instance all shared widgets auto-render inline and no coordinator is needed.' (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:175-176). e2e (test-e2e:200-225): one shared_col set to Petal.Width propagates into both x and the y-operand of the generated code, with ptr_update_plot draw and a populated picker.
findings.verification: Read e2e test single-instance-shared (test-e2e-vignette-examples-shinytest2.R:200-208): boots single-instance-shared, set_input(app,'shared_col','Petal.Width',wait=FALSE), draw(app,'ptr_update_plot') -- the STANDARD ptr_update_plot button, NOT ptr_shared_draw_all -- then asserts ptr_code contains 'Petal.Width'. Mechanism grounded in shared_partition (28-75): single formula => every shared key count 1 => all formula-local, inline id shared_<key> (canonical_shared_id, ids.R:118), driven by ptr_update_plot (ptr_controls_panel, app.R:401-448). Claim holds exactly. The roxygen-at-:175-176 line corroboration was not re-pinned but the behavior is verified from the e2e + partition + chrome source.

findings.claim: A single shared panel widget drives every panel that references its key: changing it once and clicking ptr_shared_draw_all re-renders all referencing panels with the new value in lockstep.
findings.detail: e2e l2-shared (tests/testthat/test-e2e-vignette-examples-shinytest2.R:226-255): sets the single panel widget shared_metric to Petal.Width once, clicks ptr_shared_draw_all, and asserts BOTH plot_1-ptr_code and plot_2-ptr_code contain Petal.Width with both plots rendering — one input drives both panels. app-grid-shared-added (154-176) and grid-shared-partition (296-327) assert the same lockstep-redraw contract.
findings.verification: Read e2e test l2-shared (test-e2e-vignette-examples-shinytest2.R:210-220): set_input(app,'shared_metric','Petal.Width',wait=FALSE) ONCE, draw(app,'ptr_shared_draw_all'), then asserts BOTH app$get_value(output='plot_1-ptr_code') AND 'plot_2-ptr_code' grepl('Petal.Width') -- one shared panel widget drives every referencing panel in lockstep on ptr_shared_draw_all. Exactly the claim. (My earlier-session 'unconfirmable' routing was wrong: the tests/ tree IS readable -- those reads had failed on a transient harness glitch, now confirmed by direct read.) The app-grid-shared-added / grid-shared-partition corroboration not re-read but l2-shared alone grounds the claim.

findings.claim: ptr_app_grid() still internally assembles the multi-instance shared host (ptr_shared_server + per-plot ptr_server) -- the SAME host shape as the L2 embed path -- exposing one panel for cross-formula shared widgets, a ptr_shared_draw_all button (only when formula_count>=2), and per-panel plots at id plot_<n>-ptr_plot (+ plot_N-ptr_code / plot_N-ptr_error). It is now UNEXPORTED (@keywords internal, absent from NAMESPACE): an internal helper, no longer a public L1 turn-key entry point.
findings.detail: ptr_app_grid(plots,...) calls ptr_app_grid_components() and wraps in shiny::shinyApp(parts$ui,parts$server) (R/paintr-app.R:1284-1305). The draw-all button ptr_shared_draw_all is emitted by the shared panel only when formula_count>=2L (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:425-426). e2e on app-grid-shared-added (154-176) and grid-shared-partition (296-327) assert ptr_shared_draw_all, shared_<key> panel widgets, plot_1/plot_2-ptr_plot, plot_N-ptr_code/error. The grid-shared-partition fixture header documents the same host shape as the L2 embed path.
findings.verification: Re-read R/paintr-app.R::ptr_app_grid (def 1324-1345): calls ptr_app_grid_components(plots,...) and wraps in shiny::shinyApp(ui=parts$ui, server=parts$server) -- host-assembly behavior unchanged. The draw-all button ptr_shared_draw_all is emitted by the shared panel only when obj$formula_count >= 2L (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:425-426). e2e fixtures app-grid-shared-added and grid-shared-partition assert ptr_shared_draw_all, shared_<key> widgets, plot_1/plot_2-ptr_plot, plot_N-ptr_code/error (tests/testthat/test-e2e-vignette-examples-shinytest2.R:157-173, 229ff). DRIFT (public-ness, the claim's framing): ptr_app_grid is now @keywords internal (line 1323) and is ABSENT from NAMESPACE (grep -n 'ptr_app_grid' NAMESPACE -> no match) -- it is no longer an exported public L1 entry point, only an internal helper that still exists. The 'L1 turn-key wrapper' label as a public surface is stale; the host-shape behavior it describes is intact.

findings.claim: Panel-collapsed shared ppVar/consumer column pickers (id shared_<key>) render eagerly in the always-visible standalone shared panel and are settable right after boot — unlike formula-local downstream source/consumer var pickers, which are suspended under a layer's Data subtab until shown (their e2e tests must first set <layer>_subtab='Controls'); the shared-partition tests set plot_N-shared_ax1/ax2 directly after wait_for_idle with no subtab navigation.
findings.detail: shared_panel_body_tag (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:350-434) builds the panel widgets in an always-visible wellPanel. The source/consumer downstream var pickers DO need the subtab: e2e source-dataset tests at tests/testthat/test-e2e-vignette-examples-shinytest2.R:534-562 set_input(app,'ggplot_subtab','Controls'). But the l2-shared-partition (250-294) and grid-shared-partition (296-327) tests set plot_N-shared_ax1/ax2 directly after only wait_for_idle() — NO ggplot_subtab navigation. The original finding's claim that those tests use the subtab path is FALSE; the panel-eager-vs-suspended distinction is real and source-grounded, but its cited l2-shared-partition-subtab corroboration was inaccurate.
findings.verification: shared_panel_body_tag (R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:350) builds shared_widgets eagerly via build_ui_for directly into the panel body (consumer branch 373-379, value branch 380-390) — placed in the always-visible standalone shared panel, NOT behind a renderUI/subtab. Contrast: formula-local downstream source/consumer var pickers route through renderUI under a layer's 'Data' subtab via ptr_setup_source_uis / ptr_setup_consumer_uis (R/paintr-server.R; the 'pickers under the layer Data subtab and the renderUI-built' comment at line 534; renderUI seeding at 610-623). So panel shared pickers are settable right after boot while formula-local var pickers require first showing the Data subtab. The finding already self-flagged that one cited e2e corroboration (partition tests using the subtab path) was inaccurate; that self-correction stands. server.R was touched by the safe_to_remove fix but the eager-vs-suspended distinction is unchanged. scope=campaign but the distinction is durable, source-grounded, and still saves a shinytest2 author real discovery effort (non-obvious binding-timing asymmetry). Claim holds.

findings.claim: A ptr_app never re-renders the plot/code until the Update-plot button (#ptr_update_plot) is clicked: the redraw observer gates on triggered() = (update-plot clicked OR a grid Draw-all fired OR ptr_gg_extra changed), so setting a ppVar/ppText placeholder widget alone leaves #ptr_code and #ptr_plot un-rendered — hence shinytest2 set_inputs on a placeholder must use wait_=FALSE (wait_=TRUE times out), then click the draw button.
findings.detail: R/paintr-app.R:433-437 builds the 'Update plot' actionButton id ns('ptr_update_plot'). R/paintr-server.R:1847-1861: clicked(x) requires a numeric click count>=1; triggered() reads update_clicked (input[[ns('ptr_update_plot')]]), draw_clicked (state$draw_trigger), extras_present (state$extras()) with NO || short-circuit, and the redraw observe() returns early unless triggered() is TRUE. The j11 journey test pins this: pre-click #ptr_code has no rendered code, setting both ppVar pickers without clicking still leaves it un-rendered, and only after draw('ptr_update_plot') does #ptr_code contain geom_point (test-j11-app-basic-journey.R:27-90).
findings.verification: Serena: ptr_controls_panel (R/paintr-app.R:401-448) builds shiny::actionButton(ns('ptr_update_plot'), ...). ptr_setup_runtime (R/paintr-server.R:1843-1956): clicked(x)=is.numeric(x)&&length(x)==1L&&x>=1L; triggered() reads update_clicked(input[[server_ns_fn('ptr_update_plot')]]) || draw_clicked(state$draw_trigger) || extras_present(length(state$extras())>0L), comment 'Read ALL three ... no || short-circuit'; observe_runtime() returns early unless triggered(), so setting a ppVar/ppText alone never redraws (hence shinytest2 must set placeholders wait_=FALSE then click draw). Claim holds; refs drifted (app.R:433-437 -> ptr_controls_panel:401-448; server.R:1847-1861 -> 1843-1956). j11 test corroboration not re-read but the source proves the gate.

findings.claim: The default ptr_app UI always contains fixed-id chrome regardless of placeholder count: a layer-select pickerInput (#ptr_layer_select), a type='hidden' tabsetPanel (#ptr_layer_tabset, one panel per geom layer), the Update-plot actionButton (#ptr_update_plot), and the output trio #ptr_plot (plotOutput) / #ptr_error (uiOutput) / #ptr_code (verbatimTextOutput).
findings.detail: ptr_controls_panel() (R/paintr-app.R:401-438): pickerInput ns('ptr_layer_select') (414-419), hidden tabsetPanel ns('ptr_layer_tabset') type='hidden' (420-423), actionButton ns('ptr_update_plot') (433-436). Outputs from the *_tag builders: error_slot_tag=uiOutput(ns('ptr_error')) (456-458), plot_card_tag body=plotOutput(ns('ptr_plot')) (479), code_block_tag=verbatimTextOutput(ns('ptr_code')) (543/556); ptr_outputs_panel() (564-570) assembles plot_card_tag+code_block_tag. The ids ptr_plot/ptr_error/ptr_code are the contract ptr_register_plot/_error/_code write to. e2e expects all three at boot (test-e2e:130-132).
findings.verification: Serena ptr_controls_panel body (R/paintr-app.R:401-448): shinyWidgets::pickerInput(inputId=ns('ptr_layer_select')), shiny::tabsetPanel(id=ns('ptr_layer_tabset'), type='hidden') one panel per layer, shiny::actionButton(ns('ptr_update_plot')). Output builders in same file (symbol overview): error_slot_tag(456)->uiOutput(ptr_error), plot_card_tag(474)->plotOutput(ptr_plot), code_block_tag(539)->verbatimTextOutput(ptr_code), assembled by ptr_outputs_panel(564). Ids match the central helpers (R/paintr-ids.R:109-114): ptr_id_plot='ptr_plot', ptr_id_error='ptr_error', ptr_id_code='ptr_code', ptr_id_update_plot='ptr_update_plot', ptr_id_layer_select='ptr_layer_select', ptr_id_layer_tabset='ptr_layer_tabset'. The fixed-id chrome set holds; refs drifted but verified.

findings.claim: Each placeholder widget's input id is `<parent-call>_<layer-index>[_<position>]_<keyword>_NA` keyed by the AST parent call + argument position (NOT a flat global counter); the parent_call prefix is the enclosing function name, so a ppText inside labs() is namespaced under `labs` (labs_1_ppText_NA) and a ppVar in aes() under `ggplot` (ggplot_1_1_ppVar_NA). The bare widget id is settable; the `<id>_ui` renderUI container is NOT a Shiny input (set_inputs on a _ui id errors 'Unable to find input binding').
findings.detail: app-basic (aes(ppVar,ppVar,color=ppVar)+geom_point()+labs(title=ppText)) yields ggplot_1_1/1_2/1_3_ppVar_NA (j11 sets/asserts these, test-j11:43-60) plus labs_1_ppText_NA (confirmed in test-render-preserve.R:46, test-super-pressure.R:59,652). test-id-table.R confirms parent_call=enclosing function: ppVar→'aes' (160), a ppText that is a direct layer child → the layer name (165). Filter discovered ids to drop _ui/-label suffixes.
findings.verification: j11 sets/asserts ggplot_1_1/1_2/1_3_ppVar_NA; labs_1_ppText_NA in render-preserve:46 + super-pressure:59,652 confirms labs() namespacing; id-table:160 ppVar parent_call='aes', :165 direct layer child → layer name; _ui container is a DOM id not a Shiny input.

findings.claim: Booting a ggpaintr app fixture standalone via shinytest2::AppDriver$new()/boot_vignette_app() hits testthat::skip_on_cran(), which outside a testthat reporter aborts ('On CRAN') rather than silently skipping; NOT_CRAN=true must be set (devtools::test() sets it) for the browser boot to proceed, alongside GGP_PKG=<repo root> pointing at the package source tree (DESCRIPTION present).
findings.detail: boot_vignette_app() (tests/testthat/helper-vignette-apps.R:20-39) calls testthat::skip_on_cran() (21), skip_if_not_installed('shinytest2')/'chromote' (22-23), a skip_if guard requiring the GGP_PKG source tree with comments about NOT_CRAN under --as-cran (26-32), and withr::local_envvar(GGP_PKG=pkg) (39). The helper comments confirm devtools sets NOT_CRAN=true so skip_on_cran() does not fire even under --as-cran, and the NOT_CRAN=true test_dir source-tree run is the authoritative gate where the browser tests RUN. For a manual probe outside the harness export NOT_CRAN=true alongside GGP_PKG=<repo root>.
findings.verification: boot_vignette_app: skip_on_cran 21, skip_if_not_installed 22-23, GGP_PKG/DESCRIPTION skip_if guard 26-38 with NOT_CRAN/--as-cran comments, withr::local_envvar(GGP_PKG=pkg) 39; consistent with CLAUDE.md proxy-trap docs.
